EDCS-00380337.text_edition: \n Rufinus Rufi filius Bandi Saisabro votum animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-00380337.translation: Rufinus, son of Rufus, of the Bandi tribe, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380337.summary: This inscription records Rufinus fulfilling a vow, likely related to a religious dedication.

EDCS-00380338.text_edition: \n Valgius Marci filius aram Bellonae de voto posuit\n
EDCS-00380338.translation: Valgius, son of Marcus, erected an altar to Bellona in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-00380338.summary: This inscription records Valgius dedicating an altar to the goddess Bellona to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-00380342.summary: This inscription is a fragment, likely part of a larger epitaph or dedication, naming someone as the son of Galeria.

EDCS-00380344.text_edition: \n Titus Callaeus Titi filius Galeria Niger veteranus sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380344.translation: Titus Callaeus, son of Titus, of the Galeria tribe, Niger, a veteran, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380344.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Titus Callaeus Niger, a veteran, wishing him a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-00380349.summary: This inscription likely refers to an individual named Augustalis, possibly a local magistrate or official, associated with the Mandatae tribe.

EDCS-00380350.translation: Vitulus, freedman of Aulus, an Augustalis.
EDCS-00380350.summary: This inscription identifies Vitulus, a freedman of Aulus, as an Augustalis, a civic or religious official.

EDCS-00380351.text_edition: \n Quintus Aponius Rusticus medicus ocularius Patriciensis Aponia Quinti liberta Mandata Eucharidi sorori hic siti sunt et tu et tibi\n
EDCS-00380351.translation: Quintus Aponius Rusticus, an eye doctor from the Patrician district, and Mandata, freedwoman of Quintus, to Eucharis, his sister: here lie you and yours and for you.
EDCS-00380351.summary: This complex epitaph records the burial place for Quintus Aponius Rusticus, a doctor, and Mandata, his freedwoman, and mentions Eucharis as his sister, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-00380352.text_edition: \n Caius Duccius Phoebus nnorum LXXX huic colonia Emeritensis locum sepulturae impensam funeris decrevit Iulia Restituta uxor domo Roma annorum LXXXIII hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-00380352.translation: Caius Duccius Phoebus, aged 80. The colony of Emerita decreed a burial place and funeral expenses for him. Iulia Restituta, his wife, from Rome, aged 83. Here lie you both; may the earth lie lightly on you both.
EDCS-00380352.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Caius Duccius Phoebus, who was granted a burial plot and expenses by the colony of Emerita, and his wife Iulia Restituta.

EDCS-00380353.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that begins with the name Caius Appuleius, likely the subject of a dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-00380354.summary: This inscription names Atania Agria, daughter of Lucius, likely part of a longer text such as a dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-00380355.text_edition: \n Lucio Atanio Glauci Adiecto Lucius Atanius Optatus de sua pecunia faciendum curavit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380355.translation: Lucius Atanius Optatus saw to it that this was made at his own expense for Lucius Atanius Glaucus Adiectus; he lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380355.summary: A funerary inscription recording the erection of a tomb by Lucius Atanius Optatus for Lucius Atanius Glaucus Adiectus.

EDCS-00380356.text_edition: \n Quintus Caecilius Moschus nnum XX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380356.translation: Quintus Caecilius Moschus, aged 20 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380356.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Quintus Caecilius Moschus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-00380357.text_edition: \n nurui Marcus Caecili Quintus Caeci nepoti \n
EDCS-00380357.translation: To his sister-in-law, Marcus Caecilius for his nephew Quintus Caecilius.
EDCS-00380357.summary: A dedication of a monument from Marcus Caecilius to his nephew Quintus Caecilius and sister-in-law.

EDCS-00380358.text_edition: \n Capito Maximi filio nnorum XXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380358.translation: To Capito, son of Maximuus, aged 25 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380358.summary: A funerary inscription for Capito, son of Maximuus, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-00380359.text_edition: \n Caius Cornelius Antiochus hic situs est\n
EDCS-00380359.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Gaius Cornelius Antiochus.

EDCS-00380360.text_edition: \n Iulia Cai filia Campana annorum VII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Calpurniae Marci filiae Caesiae locus Cai Iuli Campani sibi posterisque suis\n
EDCS-00380360.translation: Iulia Campana, daughter of Gaius, aged 7 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. This plot is for Calpurnia, daughter of Marcus, Caesiae, and for Gaius Iulius Campanus, for himself and his descendants.
EDCS-00380360.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulia Campana, recording her burial and reserving a burial plot for others.

EDCS-00380361.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a funerary or dedicatory text for Gaius Iulius Bal[...].

EDCS-00380362.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Rusticus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Cornelia Luci liberta Aucta uxor hic sita est ius Cai filius Lucius Senius Dirtanus Bilbilitanus hic situs est S \n
EDCS-00380362.translation: Gaius Iulius Rusticus lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Aucta, freedwoman of Lucius, wife of Cornelia Lucius, lies here. Her son Gaius, Lucius Senius Dirtanus of Bilbilis, lies here.
EDCS-00380362.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Gaius Iulius Rusticus, his wife Aucta, and their son Lucius Senius Dirtanus.

EDCS-00380363.text_edition: \n Lucius Minatius Genialis annorum LXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Arria Pale uxor locum sepulturae permissu A\n
EDCS-00380363.translation: Lucius Minatius Genialis, aged 65 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Arria Pale, wife, with permission of A[...], the burial place.
EDCS-00380363.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Minatius Genialis, aged 65, and his wife Arria Pale, mentioning permission for the burial plot.

EDCS-00380364.text_edition: \n Petronia Marci filia Quadratilla annorum XXV Lucio Servilio Galatae Servilia Luci filia Iucunda \n
EDCS-00380364.translation: Petronia Quadratilla, daughter of Marcus, aged 25 years. Lucius Galata, son of Servilius. Servilia Iucunda, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-00380364.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning Petronia Quadratilla, Lucius Servilius Galatas, and Servilia Iucunda, possibly related to a shared monument.

EDCS-00380365.text_edition: \n Helpis Marci Popilli Iunci serva nnorum XXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380365.translation: Helpis, slave woman of Marcus Popillius Iuncus, aged 22, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380365.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Helpis, a slave woman, with a standard epitaph formula.

EDCS-00380366.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly recording the name of a person named Memorinus, associated with Marcus Popillius Iuncus.

EDCS-00380367.text_edition: \n Therpsis Gai Fabi Fraterni serva nnorum XXVI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380367.translation: Therpsis, slave woman of Gaius Fabius Frater, aged 26, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380367.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Therpsis, a slave woman, with a standard epitaph formula.

EDCS-00380368.text_edition: \n Q AN Val M filio piissimo \n
EDCS-00380368.translation: To Aulus, son of Numerius, most dutiful.
EDCS-00380368.summary: This is a dedication to Aulus, son of Numerius, possibly on a tomb or a public monument, indicating his filial piety.

EDCS-00380369.text_edition: \n Titus Vinucius Felix Minicia Quinta uxor hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis Lucius Scantius Acutus Scantia Melyboea hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis Ianuaria Scantiae Meliboeae filia Urbanus Scanti Acuti filius hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-00380369.translation: Titus Vinucius Felix and Minicia Quinta, his wife, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lucius Scantius Acutus and Scantia Melyboea are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Ianuaria, daughter of Scantia Melyboea, and Urbanus, son of Scantius Acutus, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380369.summary: This is a multi-person tombstone recording the burial of several individuals and their familial relationships, with standard epitaph formulas.

EDCS-00380370.text_edition: \n Caius Domitius Pylades medicus annorum LV hic situs est te rogo praeteriens ut dicas sit tibi terra levis Cornelia Marci liberta Maurilla uxor Caius Domitius Cai libertus Restitutus Lucius Valerius Reburrus missicius nnorum LXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis et Attiae Libadi uxori dulcissimae Fausta Coelia hic sita Caius Nonius Batullus hic situs Atlia Specia hic sita meas uxores Lucius Iustulenus Chrestus Helvia Aucta Lucius Peticius Felix Iulia Apana Publius Curtius Phoebus sibi et Scantiae Bellae Iuliae Maurae Ascanius nnorum XXIX Pastorilla annorum XXV Echion annorum II hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis Naevidia Optata Cai Naevidi Optati filia hic sita est annorum XX sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380370.translation: Gaius Domitius Pylades, a doctor, aged 55, is buried here. I ask you, passerby, as you go by, to say: May the earth lie lightly on you. Cornelia Marci, freedwoman of Marcus, his wife Maurilla. Gaius Domitius Restitutus, freedman of Caius. Lucius Valerius Reburrus, a soldier, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. And to Attia Libas, his dearest wife. Fausta Coelia is buried here. Gaius Nonius Batullus is buried here. Atlia Specia is buried here. My wives. Lucius Iustulenus Chrestus and Helvia Aucta. Lucius Peticius Felix and Iulia Apana. Publius Curtius Phoebus for himself and for Scantia Bella. Iulia Maura and Ascanius, aged 29. Pastorilla, aged 25. Echion, aged 2, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Naevidia Optata, daughter of Caius Naevidius Optatus, is buried here, aged 20. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380370.summary: This extensive tombstone records multiple burials, including a doctor, freedmen, soldiers, wives, and children, with a variety of epitaph formulas and pleas to passersby.

EDCS-00380371.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely part of a name and patronymic or family name, possibly from a tombstone.

EDCS-00380372.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording the name Amoena and her father's name, Quintus, possibly part of a funerary context.

EDCS-00380381.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Nonius Marinus annorum LXX hic situs est Marcia Secundilla marito pientissimo fecit et sibi Marcia Secundilla nnorum sibi et marito pientissimo fecit hic sita est sit terra levis\n
EDCS-00380381.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Nonius Marinus, aged 70, is buried here. Marcia Secundilla made this for her most devoted husband and for herself. Marcia Secundilla, aged [...], made this for herself and for her most devoted husband. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-00380381.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Nonius Marinus and his wife Marcia Secundilla, who erected the monument for both of them.

EDCS-00380383.text_edition: \n Marcus Rufeus |(mulieris) libertus locus pedum X\n
EDCS-00380383.translation: Marcus Rufeus, freedman of the woman, a plot of 10 feet.
EDCS-00380383.summary: This inscription records the purchase or allocation of burial space (a plot of 10 feet) for Marcus Rufeus, a freedman, possibly indicating ownership or rights.

EDCS-00380387.text_edition: \n Gaius Iulius Pelecus Lacibaea votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380387.translation: Gaius Iulius Pelecus Lacibaea willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380387.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Iulius Pelecus Lacibaea, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380388.text_edition: \n Lanci annorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis pater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-00380388.translation: Lanci, aged 50, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you, father. His father had this made.
EDCS-00380388.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lanci, aged 50, set up by his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380389.text_edition: \n Cilia Mal-vi filia hic sita est an<n>orum \n
EDCS-00380389.translation: Cilia, daughter of Malvus, aged [X], is buried here.
EDCS-00380389.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cilia, daughter of Malvus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380390.text_edition: \n Caecilia Quinti filia Anus hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380390.translation: Anus, daughter of Quitus Caecilius, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380390.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Anus, daughter of Quitus Caecilius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380400.translation: To the departed spirits of Metturonus, aged [XX].
EDCS-00380400.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirit of Metturonus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380448.text_edition: \n Balaesus Antuti filius annorum XX hic situs est\n
EDCS-00380448.translation: Balaesus, son of Antutus, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-00380448.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Balaesus, son of Antutus, aged 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380449.text_edition: \n Montanus Ciloni annorum LXXV hic situs tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380449.translation: Montanus, aged 75, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380449.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Montanus, aged 75, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380450.text_edition: \n Cloutio Ama nnrum LXXXV hic situs tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380450.translation: Cloutius, aged 85, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380450.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cloutius, aged 85, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380451.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus No annorum hic situs sit terra levis\n
EDCS-00380451.translation: To the departed spirits of No, aged [XX]; may the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-00380451.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirit of No, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380452.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits of Mi.
EDCS-00380452.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits of Mi, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380453.text_edition: \n Lucius Quadrati filius annorum XXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380453.translation: Lucius, son of Quadratus, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380453.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius, son of Quadratus, who died at age 35.

EDCS-00380456.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Avitus.
EDCS-00380456.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld by Avitus.

EDCS-00380458.text_edition: \n Noanius annorum LX hic situs sit terra levis\n
EDCS-00380458.translation: Noanius, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380458.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Noanius, who died at age 60.

EDCS-00380459.text_edition: \n Caerus Triti filius annorum LX hic situs tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380459.translation: Caerus, son of Tritus, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380459.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caerus, son of Tritus, who died at age 60.

EDCS-00380462.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus us annorum XXII hic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380462.translation: [Name], aged 22, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380462.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed individual who died at age 22.

EDCS-00380465.text_edition: \n Grano Aroni annorum LX hic situs tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380465.translation: Grano, son of Aaron, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380465.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Grano, son of Aaron, who died at age 60.

EDCS-00380467.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Paterno Paterni nnorum LXXX\n
EDCS-00380467.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Paterno, son of Paternus, aged 80.
EDCS-00380467.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for Paterno, son of Paternus, who died at age 80.

EDCS-00380468.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Festus Festini filius nnum \n
EDCS-00380468.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Festus, son of Festinus, aged [XX].
EDCS-00380468.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for Festus, son of Festinus, whose age is partially preserved.

EDCS-00380469.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Reb[urr------
EDCS-00380469.summary: This is a partial funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for an individual whose name is lost.

EDCS-00380470.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Elesus Paterni annorum XXXX hic tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380470.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Elesus, son of Paternus, aged 40, may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-00380470.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Elesus, son of Paternus, who died at age 40.

EDCS-00380471.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum MaII nnorum XIIII hic situs sit terra levis\n
EDCS-00380471.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. MaII, aged fourteen, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-00380471.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of a young person named MaII, with a wish for their repose.

EDCS-00380474.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caesulla Caesi filia annorum XV hic sita tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380474.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Caesulla, daughter of Caesus, aged fifteen, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-00380474.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of a young girl named Caesulla, daughter of Caesus.

EDCS-00380475.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus o annorum XIII Dis Manibus Coni annorum IIIII \n
EDCS-00380475.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Aged] thirteen. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Coni, aged five.
EDCS-00380475.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription mentioning two individuals, one aged thirteen and another named Coni, aged five.

EDCS-00380477.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Ama annorum XXX \n
EDCS-00380477.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ama, aged thirty.
EDCS-00380477.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Ama at the age of thirty.

EDCS-00380479.text_edition: \n Possuit sic Reburro nnorum LX hic situs tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380479.translation: Possessed by Reburrus, aged sixty. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-00380479.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Reburrus, aged sixty.

EDCS-00380480.text_edition: \n ------] R Eburni filius annorum LX hic situs est tibi terra\n
EDCS-00380480.translation: [---] son of Eburnus, aged sixty. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-00380480.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of a man, son of Eburnus, aged sixty.

EDCS-00380481.text_edition: \n Tridius ni filius nnorum X hic situs tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380481.translation: Tridius, son of [---], aged ten. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-00380481.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of a young boy named Tridius, aged ten.

EDCS-00380482.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Caerus at the age of sixty-three.

EDCS-00380484.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Dobiteinae annorum XX hic sita tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380484.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Dobiteina, aged twenty, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-00380484.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of a young woman named Dobiteina, aged twenty.

EDCS-00380485.text_edition: \n I Doitena Caeni annorum L hic sita tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380485.translation: I Doitena, daughter of Caenus, aged fifty. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-00380485.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Doitena, daughter of Caenus, aged fifty.

EDCS-00380486.text_edition: \n S CIN LOC ANE QVM annorum LV- hic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-00380486.translation: To S. Cin. Locane, who lived 55 years, may the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-00380486.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for S. Cin. Locane, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00700022.translation: To the deified Augustus, Lucius Papirius, son of Lucius, Flamen Augustalis of the province of Lusitania.
EDCS-00700022.summary: This is a dedication to the deified Augustus by Lucius Papirius, a Flamen Augustalis of Lusitania, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-02400029.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo C Tanginus Tonginae filius votum piissime factum solvit\n
EDCS-02400029.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, C. Tanginus, son of Tongina, most piously fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-02400029.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by C. Tanginus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000430.text_edition: \n Signum Marti Augusto Cicerius Iuvenalis Augustalis ex testamento poni iussit Masclionus sive Raicirri ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-03000430.translation: Cicerius Iuvenalis, Augustalis, ordered this statue of Mars Augustus to be set up by his will; Masclionus, also known as Raicirri, saw to its placement.
EDCS-03000430.summary: This is a dedication of a statue of Mars Augustus, commissioned by Cicerius Iuvenalis and executed by Masclionus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000436.text_edition: \n Victoriae Curius Privatus votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-03000436.translation: Curius Privatus willingly fulfilled his vow to Victory.
EDCS-03000436.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Victoria, dedicated by Curius Privatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000437.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum AME RVF CE annorum XXXV\n
EDCS-03000437.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, AME RVF CE, aged 35 years.
EDCS-03000437.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for AME RVF CE, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000439.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cainus, son of Catur, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000440.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Celleae annorum LXV\n
EDCS-03000440.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, Cellea, aged 65 years.
EDCS-03000440.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cellea, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000442.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Valeriae Amoenae annorum LXX filio sic fecit\n
EDCS-03000442.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, Valeria Amoena, aged 70 years; her son made this.
EDCS-03000442.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Amoena, erected by her son, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000443.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum F FV annorum XXX\n
EDCS-03000443.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, F FV, aged 30 years.
EDCS-03000443.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for F FV, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03000453.text_edition: \n Gaius Matius Gai libertus Optatus nnorum LXX Matia Gai liberta Festa nnorum Matia Optata annorum XXV Caius Matius Emeritus annorum XVI ex testamento hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-03000453.translation: Gaius Matius Optatus, freedman of Gaius, aged 70, and Matia Festa, freedwoman of Gaius, aged [---], and Matia Optata, aged 25, and Caius Matius Emeritus, aged 16, are buried here according to the will. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03000453.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for four individuals, Gaius Matius Optatus, Matia Festa, Matia Optata, and Caius Matius Emeritus, with the burial arranged by will, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000456.text_edition: \n Severa Tancini filia annorum L hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Sextus Iulius Hispanus piae uxori \n
EDCS-03000456.translation: Severa, daughter of Tancinus, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sextus Iulius Hispanus placed this for his devoted wife.
EDCS-03000456.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Severa, wife of Sextus Iulius Hispanus, and states she was 50 years old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300382.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Constantino nobilissimo Caesari ac fortissimo \n
EDCS-03300382.translation: To our lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus, most noble Caesar and bravest...
EDCS-03300382.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely dedicating a monument or honor to the Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus, who reigned as Augustus from 324 to 337 AD.

EDCS-03300389.text_edition: \n Titus Iunius Titi filius tus mater ponendum curavit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03300389.translation: Titus Iunius, son of Titus, his mother took care to place this; he is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03300389.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Iunius, placed by his mother, recording his burial, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300406.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely the names of a person or persons, possibly on a tombstone or a marker, from the Late Antique or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-03300410.text_edition: \n Caius N et I M hic situs est\n
EDCS-03300410.translation: Caius N and I M are buried here.
EDCS-03300410.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Caius N and I M, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300411.text_edition: \n Lucius Norbanus Sillo annorum L hic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03300411.translation: Lucius Norbanus Sillo, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03300411.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Lucius Norbanus Sillo, who was 50 years old, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300413.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a child named Secunda, aged 3, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300414.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Norbana Bovia annorum LXXXXVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis filius pientissimus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-03300414.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Norbana Bovia, aged 98, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her most devoted son took care to make this.
EDCS-03300414.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Norbana Bovia, aged 98, placed by her devoted son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700348.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Cominio Rufo Galeria annorum LI Lucretia Saletina mater filio pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-03700348.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Cominius Rufus, aged [---]. Galeria Lucretia Saletina, his mother, placed this for her most devoted son.
EDCS-03700348.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaius Cominius Rufus, placed by his mother, Galeria Lucretia Saletina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03700354.text_edition: \n LEA Iunia et Sabina Secunda Cassia Turran[ia ------\n
EDCS-03700354.translation: LEA Iunia and Sabina Secunda Cassia Turran[ia...]
EDCS-03700354.summary: This inscription likely records the names of women, possibly for a funerary monument, with incomplete names and a lacuna.

EDCS-03700357.translation: Oipaengia Apana Pissiri fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-03700357.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Oipaengia Apana Pissiri fulfilled a vow, possibly dedicated to a deity.

EDCS-03700363.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-03700363.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Aurelius Carinus, a Caesar, likely for a public monument or honor.

EDCS-03700368.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Ebriliae Cai libertae \n
EDCS-03700368.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, to Ebrilia, freedwoman of Caius.
EDCS-03700368.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Ebrilia, a freedwoman, by an unknown person or persons.

EDCS-03700369.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Argentariae Veranae Emeritensi nnorum LXV Argentarius Vegetinus materterae et patronae faciendum curavit hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700369.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, to Argentaria Verana of Emerita, aged 65. Argentarius Vegetinus saw to its construction for his aunt and patroness. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700369.summary: This funerary inscription honors Argentaria Verana, who died at 65, and was erected by her nephew and patron, Argentarius Vegetinus, in Emerita.

EDCS-03700371.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quinto Ursio Paederoti annorum LX Ursia Verana patri piissimo faciendum curavit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700371.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, to Quintus Ursio Paederotus, aged 60. Ursia Verana erected this for her most dutiful father. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700371.summary: This funerary inscription honors Quintus Ursio Paederotus, who died at 60, and was erected by his daughter Ursia Verana.

EDCS-03700372.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Philetus Phileti filius nnorum VIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-03700372.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, Philetus, son of Philetus, aged 8. He lies here.
EDCS-03700372.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Philetus, aged 8, son of Philetus.

EDCS-03700373.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Glyconis natione Nicomedica nnorum XXXXV sit tibi terra levis Lucius Munatius Asclepiades et Marcus Lucanius Avitus filii matri piissimae fecerunt\n
EDCS-03700373.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, to Iulia, of Nicomedia, aged 45. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Lucius Munatius Asclepiades and Marcus Lucanius Avitus, her sons, made this for their most dutiful mother.
EDCS-03700373.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia, a 45-year-old woman from Nicomedia, and was erected by her sons.

EDCS-03700374.text_edition: \n Publius Curtius Cypaerus hic situs est Publius Curtius Publi filius Papiria Italicus annorum XXXVIII\n
EDCS-03700374.translation: Publius Curtius Cypaerus lies here, son of Publius Curtius, of the Papiria tribe, aged 38.
EDCS-03700374.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Publius Curtius Cypaerus, aged 38, son of Publius Curtius.

EDCS-03700375.text_edition: \n Valeria Allage nnorum LX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Caius Sulpicius Cai filius Galeria Superstes annorum XXXVIII IIvir III Metellinensium huic colonia Emeritensis et colonia Metellinensis decreto decurionum locum sepulturae et funeris inpensam decreverunt hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700375.translation: Valeria Allage, aged 60, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Caius Sulpicius Superstes, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 38, twice chief magistrate of the Metellinienses, was granted a burial place and funeral expenses by the colony of Emerita and the colony of Metellinenses by decree of the decurions. He lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700375.summary: This funerary inscription honors Valeria Allage, aged 60, and Caius Sulpicius Superstes, aged 38, a magistrate, for whom the colonies of Emerita and Metellinenses provided burial honors.

EDCS-03700376.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Attio Quintilio annorum LXV sit tibi terra levis Memmius Suavis privignus et Memmia Suavola uxor faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-03700376.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Attius Quintilius, aged 65, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Memmius Suavis, his stepson, and Memmia Suavola, his wife, saw to the construction.
EDCS-03700376.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Attius Quintilius, erected by his stepson and wife.

EDCS-03700377.text_edition: \n Vernaclus Agroeci dispensatoris vicarius nnorum XXII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700377.translation: Vernaclus, assistant to the steward Agroecus, aged 22, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700377.summary: A tombstone for Vernaclus, an assistant steward, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700378.text_edition: \n Cornelia Publi liberta Nothis secunda mima Sollemnis et Halyi hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700378.translation: Cornelia Nothis, freedwoman of Publius, a dancer named Secunda, lies here with Sollemnis and Halyus. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700378.summary: A tombstone for Cornelia Nothis, a freedwoman and actress, along with two others.

EDCS-03700380.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Egnatio Gallieno Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo Dacico maximo Germanico maximo tribunicia potestate X consuli III imperatori III proconsuli patri patriae Publius Clodius Laetus Macrinus vir clarissimus legatus eorum pro praetore devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-03700380.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Egnatius Gallienus Pius Felix Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, greatest of Dacians, greatest of Germans, with tribunician power for the 10th time, consul for the 3rd time, Imperator for the 3rd time, former proconsul, father of the fatherland. Publius Clodius Laetus Macrinus, most illustrious man, his legate pro praetore, devoted to his divine will and majesty.
EDCS-03700380.summary: An inscription dedicating a monument to Emperor Gallienus by a prominent official, Publius Clodius Laetus Macrinus, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03700381.text_edition: \n Quintus Caecilius Quinti filius Papiria Varica cohortis Antistianae praetoriae Titus Caecilius Quinti filius Papiria Calventius Caecilia Titi liberta Urbana hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-03700381.translation: Quintus Caecilius Varica, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, of the Antistian praetorian cohort, and Titus Caecilius Calventius, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, and Urbana, freedwoman of Titus, lie here.
EDCS-03700381.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Quintus Caecilius Varica and Titus Caecilius Calventius along with Urbana, a freedwoman.

EDCS-03700382.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Acilia Apolauste nnorum XXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Titinius Lupus Emeritensis uxori et Lucius Acilius Apolaustus sorori et sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-03700382.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Acilia Apolauste, aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Titinius Lupus of Emerita and Lucius Acilius Apolaustus, for his sister and himself, made this.
EDCS-03700382.summary: A tombstone for Acilia Apolauste, erected by her husband Titinius Lupus and brother Lucius Acilius Apolaustus.

EDCS-03700385.text_edition: \n PONTINA Publi filia Qua<r=N>ta hic sita est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-03700385.translation: Pontina, daughter of Publius Quareta, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700385.summary: A tombstone for Pontina, daughter of Publius Quareta.

EDCS-03700392.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum ECannorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700392.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [Name lost], aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700392.summary: A tombstone with a missing name, recording the burial of a 50-year-old individual.

EDCS-03700394.text_edition: \n Hermeros annorum II hic situs est sit tibi terra levis ter f(aciendum)? c(uravit)\n
EDCS-03700394.translation: Hermeros, aged 2, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Ter [faciendum?] [curavit?]
EDCS-03700394.summary: A tombstone for Hermeros, a child aged 2, with an unclear indication of who supervised the construction.

EDCS-03700395.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius filius Papiria Rufus annorum C hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Rufilla filia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-03700395.translation: Caius Iulius Rufus, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, aged 100, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Rufilla, his daughter, saw to the construction.
EDCS-03700395.summary: A tombstone for Caius Iulius Rufus, who lived to be 100 years old, erected by his daughter Rufilla.

EDCS-03700396.text_edition: \n VLI annorum LX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis filius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-03700396.translation: VLI, aged sixty years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. His son saw to the construction.
EDCS-03700396.summary: This is a funerary inscription for VLI, who died at age sixty, erected by his son.

EDCS-03700400.text_edition: \n Marcus Iulius Arcisus valia uxor de suo faciendum curavit Iulia Marci filia Paula sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700400.translation: Marcus Iulius Arcisus. His wife Valia, with her own resources, saw to the construction. Iulia Paula, daughter of Marcus. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700400.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Iulius Arcisus, erected by his wife Valia, with a blessing for the deceased.

EDCS-03700401.text_edition: \n Caecilia Cai filia Galla annorum XX hic sita est\n
EDCS-03700401.translation: Caecilia Galla, daughter of Caius, aged twenty years, is buried here.
EDCS-03700401.summary: This is a tombstone for Caecilia Galla, who died at age twenty.

EDCS-03700406.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cilia.
EDCS-03700406.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for Cilia.

EDCS-03700407.text_edition: \n Caecilia Quinti filia Tertia annorum XLV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700407.translation: Caecilia Tertia, daughter of Quintus, aged forty-five years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700407.summary: This funerary inscription records Caecilia Tertia, who died at age forty-five.

EDCS-03700411.text_edition: \n Lucius Naevidius Cai filius Niger annorum LXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700411.translation: Lucius Naevidius Niger, son of Caius, aged seventy years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03700411.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Naevidius Niger, who died at age seventy.

EDCS-03700413.text_edition: \n Scaeva Glouti filia hic situs est sit tibi terra levis filius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-03700413.translation: Scaeva, daughter of Gloutus, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Her son saw to the construction.
EDCS-03700413.summary: This funerary monument records Scaeva, daughter of Gloutus, erected by her son.

EDCS-03700414.text_edition: \n Marcus Vibius Rufus Papiria annorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis AIAI\n
EDCS-03700414.translation: Marcus Vibius Rufus Papiria, aged fifty years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. [untranslatable letters].
EDCS-03700414.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Vibius Rufus Papiria, who died at age fifty.

EDCS-03700427.text_edition: \n Tancinus Cauquiri filius annorum LXXXV hic situs est tibi terra levis frater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-03700427.translation: Tancinus, son of Cauquirus, aged eighty-five years, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. His brother saw to the construction.
EDCS-03700427.summary: This funerary inscription records Tancinus, son of Cauquirus, who died at age eighty-five, erected by his brother.

EDCS-03700431.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Ses[ti...].
EDCS-03700431.summary: This inscription begins a dedication to the underworld spirits for Lucius Sestius, with the rest of the text missing.

EDCS-03700438.text_edition: \n Ante V passuum milia terminus limitis V ante IV Vinia Campegiensis et Valseni\n
EDCS-03700438.translation: Boundary stone for the fifth milepost from the fifth mile from Vinia Campegiensis and Valseni.
EDCS-03700438.summary: This is a boundary stone marking land ownership or a road, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-04900488.text_edition: \n Lucio Antonio Urso liberti et familia\n
EDCS-04900488.translation: To Lucius Antonius Urso, from his freedmen and household.
EDCS-04900488.summary: This is a dedication from freedmen and their families to their former master, Lucius Antonius Urso, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-04900537.text_edition: \n Adia Clouti filia annorum LX hic sita tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-04900537.translation: Adia Clouti, aged 60, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-04900537.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Adia Clouti, a woman of 60, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-04900554.text_edition: \n Celtius Clouti filius nnorum XXXII hic situs sit terra levis\n
EDCS-04900554.translation: Celtius Clouti, aged 32, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-04900554.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Celtius Clouti, a man of 32, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-04900568.text_edition: \n Nymphis Augustis sacrum Marcus Antonius libens votum animo solvit\n
EDCS-04900568.translation: Sacred to the August Gods. Marcus Antonius willingly fulfilled his vow with a willing spirit.
EDCS-04900568.summary: This is a religious dedication to the August Gods by Marcus Antonius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-04900571.text_edition: \n deo Lutono votum solvit merito libens\n
EDCS-04900571.translation: Willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the god Lutunus.
EDCS-04900571.summary: This is a religious votive inscription to the god Lutunus, likely from Roman Britain during the imperial period.

EDCS-05000393.summary: This is a funerary epitaph or record for Theseus, aged 45, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000399.text_edition: \n Caius Licinius Vegetus Quangeio Turicaeco votum libens animo solvit\n
EDCS-05000399.translation: Gaius Licinius Vegetus willingly and with a willing spirit fulfilled his vow to Quangeius Turicaecus.
EDCS-05000399.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Licinius Vegetus to Quangeius Turicaecus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000400.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Terentiae Lauri filiae nnorum XXXI Iunia mater filiae pientissimae ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-05000400.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iunia, the most devoted mother, arranged for the burial of her daughter Terentia Lauria, aged 31.
EDCS-05000400.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Terentia Lauria, erected by her devoted mother Iunia, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000402.text_edition: \n Doquirus Bovi votum Iovi libens merito\n
EDCS-05000402.translation: Doquirus, Bovi, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-05000402.summary: This is a religious votive inscription by Doquirus Bovi to Jupiter, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000413.text_edition: \n Bucco Maelonis filius Cauriensis annorum XXV hic situs est \n
EDCS-05000413.translation: Bucco, son of Maelo, from Cauria, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-05000413.summary: This is a tombstone for Bucco, son of Maelo, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-05000414.text_edition: \n Proculus Luci libertus annorum V hic situs est Lucius Quintius ulus et Titiana filio faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-05000414.translation: Proculus, freedman of Lucius, aged 5, is buried here. Lucius Quintius Ulus and Titiana had this made for their son.
EDCS-05000414.summary: This tombstone records the death of Proculus, freedman of Lucius, at the age of 5, and was commissioned by his parents Lucius Quintius Ulus and Titiana.

EDCS-05000421.text_edition: \n Celtiatus Veniati hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05000421.translation: Celtiatu, son of Veniatus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05000421.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Celtiatu, son of Veniatus, with a common epitaph wishing him a light earth.

EDCS-05000425.text_edition: \n Amoena AEEI filia annorum LV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Salius verna faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05000425.translation: Amoena, daughter of Aeeius, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Salius, a slave, had this made.
EDCS-05000425.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Amoena, daughter of Aeeius, aged 55, and notes that it was commissioned by her slave Salius.

EDCS-05000427.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Novatus Boeli filius vixsit sic annos LXXX hic situs sit tibi terra levis Calus filius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05000427.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Novatus, son of Boelius, lived for 80 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Calus, his son, had this made.
EDCS-05000427.summary: This tombstone honors Novatus, son of Boelius, who lived 80 years, and was commissioned by his son Calus.

EDCS-05000430.text_edition: \n Fabi Pisini Arronis Lerogum annorum XXXV \n
EDCS-05000430.translation: Fabi, son of Pisinius Arro, from Lerogum, aged 35.
EDCS-05000430.summary: This is a fragmented inscription recording the name and age of Fabi, son of Pisinius Arro.

EDCS-05200345.text_edition: \n Lucio filio Camalus filius Pinara et Apana Luci filia et Pisira Bouti uxsor sic et Cabura Caenonis mater curaverunt\n
EDCS-05200345.translation: For Lucius, their son. Camalus, son of Pinara, and Apana, daughter of Lucius, and Boutis' wife Pisira, and Cabura, mother of Caenon, took care of this.
EDCS-05200345.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by several women for a deceased man named Lucius, specifying familial relationships.

EDCS-05500008.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo in memoriam Luci Atili Maximi Severiani filii pientissimi Lucius Atilius Atilianus et Artullia Cai filia Severa ex argenti libris posuerunt\n
EDCS-05500008.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. In memory of Lucius Atilius Maximus Severianus, most devoted son. Lucius Atilius Atilianus and Artullia Severa, daughter of Caius, set this up from the silver of their inheritance.
EDCS-05500008.summary: This dedication to Jupiter was set up by Lucius Atilius Atilianus and Artullia Severa in memory of their devoted son, Lucius Atilius Maximus Severianus.

EDCS-05500032.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Flavia Rufina, daughter of Lucius, priestess of the province of Lusitania and also perpetual priestess of the colony of Emerita and the municipality of Salacia, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05500032.summary: This dedication to Jupiter was made by Flavia Rufina, a prominent priestess of several communities, by official decree.

EDCS-05500047.translation: For Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Father of the Fatherland. The colony of Pax Iulia, by decree of the decurions, [dedicated this] under Quintus Petronius Maternus and Gaius Julius Iulianus, IIvirs.
EDCS-05500047.summary: This is a public dedication by the colony of Pax Iulia to Lucius Aelius Aurelius Commodus, son of Emperor Antoninus Pius, during the duumvirate of Petronius Maternus and Julius Iulianus.

EDCS-05500133.translation: Gaius Julius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, arranged for this to be made for his wife Luria Boutia, daughter of Titus, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-05500133.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband to his wife, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05500168.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, son of the deified Antoninus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul II, father of the country, from the municipals of Ammaia.
EDCS-05500168.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Lucius Verus, likely from the 2nd century CE, by the citizens of Ammaia.

EDCS-05500170.text_edition: \n Gaio Iulio Vegeto flamini proviniciae Lusitaniae Propinia Severa marito optimo\n
EDCS-05500170.translation: To Gaius Julius Vegetus, flamen of the province of Lusitania, from Propinia Severa, his most excellent husband.
EDCS-05500170.summary: This is an epitaph honoring a flamen of Lusitania, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05500173.translation: Optata, freedwoman of Aelius Scapula from Clunia, lies here.
EDCS-05500173.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedwoman, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05500182.translation: To Gaius Ummidius Durmius Quadratus, legate of Gaius Caesar Germanicus Imperator, proconsul. By my soul's judgment, the oath of the Aritiensians: I will be an enemy to those whom I know to be enemies to Gaius Caesar Germanicus, and if anyone brings or has brought danger to him or his safety, I will not cease to pursue them by armed, internecine war by land and sea until they pay their penalties to him; nor will I hold my children dearer than his safety, and I will consider those who have been hostile towards him as enemies to me. If knowingly I lie or have lied, then may Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and the deified Augustus, and all other immortal gods make me and my children an exile from our fatherland, safety, and all fortunes. On the fifth day before the Ides of May, in the old town of Aritium, under the consulship of Gnaeus Aceronius Proculus and Gaius Petronius Pontius Nigrinus, with Vegetus Talticus and Vibius Arion as magistrates.
EDCS-05500182.summary: This is a civic oath sworn by the people of Aritium to Gaius Ummidius Durmius Quadratus, a legate, pledging loyalty to Emperor Gaius Caesar Germanicus, likely from the reign of Tiberius (14-37 CE).

EDCS-05500185.translation: Sacred. Marcus Afranius Euporio and Lucius Fabius Dananus, Augustales, gave this gift to the municipality.
EDCS-05500185.summary: This is a dedication to Aesculapius by two Augustales, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05500189.translation: Flavia Tyche, cernophora, to the Great Mother of the Gods, Cybele of Phrygia, through Marcus Julius Cassianus and Cassia Severa, in the consulship of Marcus Attilius and Annius Gallus.
EDCS-05500189.summary: This is a dedication to the Phrygian Mother Goddess by Flavia Tyche, likely from the late 1st century CE.

EDCS-05500193.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-05500193.translation: To Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Claudius, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar, great-great-grandson of the deified Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power III, imperator III, consul II, designated III. Gaius Heius Primus Cato, perpetual Augustalis, gifted the proscenium and orchestra with their decorations. HEIA [gifted].
EDCS-05500193.summary: This is a dedication of public works to Emperor Nero by a prominent citizen, likely from the early to mid-1st century CE.

EDCS-05500197.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, grandson of the deified Pius, great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, Lucius Aurelius Commodus Augustus Germanicus Sarmaticus. Felicitas Iulia Olisipo, through Quintus Coelius Cassianus and Marcus Fabius Tuscum, IIviri.
EDCS-05500197.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Commodus by the city of Felicitas Iulia Olisipo (Lisbon), likely from the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05500200.translation: To Lucius Caecilius Celer Rectus, son of Lucius, quaestor of the province of Baetica, tribune of the plebs, praetor. Felicitas Iulia Olisipo.
EDCS-05500200.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Caecilius Celer Rectus, a Roman official, by the city of Felicitas Iulia Olisipo (Lisbon), likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05500201.translation: The Baths of Cassius, rebuilt from the ground up by order of Numerius Albanus, a man of highest rank, governor of the province of Lusitania, under the supervision of Aurelius Firmus, during the consulship of Nepotianus and Facundus.
EDCS-05500201.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of the Baths of Cassius, commissioned by the governor Numerius Albanus and supervised by Aurelius Firmus, with the date indicated by the consuls.

EDCS-05500205.text_edition: \n Flaminicae provinciae Lusitaniae Serviliae Luci filiae Albini decreto decurionum Lucceiae Quinti filiae Albinae Terentiani decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-05500205.translation: To Servilia Lucilla, daughter of Lucius, daughter of Albinus, Flaminica of the province of Lusitania, by decree of the decurions; to Lucceia Albina, daughter of Quintus, daughter of Terentianus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-05500205.summary: This inscription honors two women, Servilia Lucilla and Lucceia Albina, as Flaminicae of Lusitania, as decreed by the local council (decurions).

EDCS-05500207.text_edition: \n Gelliae Quinti filiae Vegetae flaminicae Marcus Gellinus Rutilianus maritus\n
EDCS-05500207.translation: To Gellia Veta, daughter of Quintus, Flaminica, by her husband Marcus Gellinus Rutilianus.
EDCS-05500207.summary: This is a dedication to Gellia Veta, a Flaminica, made by her husband Marcus Gellinus Rutilianus.

EDCS-05500270.text_edition: \n Lucius Iulius Maelo Caudicus flamen divi Augusti de suo fecit\n
EDCS-05500270.translation: Lucius Iulius Maelo Caudicus erected this at his own expense as priest of the deified Augustus.
EDCS-05500270.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Lucius Iulius Maelo Caudicus, who served as a priest of the deified Augustus, at his own expense.

EDCS-05500347.text_edition: \n Fortunae sacrum Frontonius Avitus animo libens posuit\n
EDCS-05500347.translation: Sacred to Fortuna, Frontonius Avitus gladly set this up.
EDCS-05500347.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Fortuna by Frontonius Avitus.

EDCS-05500353.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Tiberius Claudius Maximus, of the cavalry wing, [died...]
EDCS-05500353.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Maximus, who served in a cavalry unit, intended for the underworld spirits.

EDCS-05500360.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Licinia Flacila had this erected for her most dutiful daughter Severia Rufina, aged 15.
EDCS-05500360.summary: This funerary inscription records that Licinia Flacila erected a tomb for her 15-year-old daughter, Severia Rufina.

EDCS-05500364.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Iulio Maximo Galeria Nepoti Afro oratori Quintus Iulius Maximus pater filio piissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05500364.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Quintus Iulius Maximus had this made for his most dutiful son, Quintus Iulius Maximus, orator.
EDCS-05500364.summary: This funerary inscription records that Quintus Iulius Maximus commissioned a monument for his son, also named Quintus Iulius Maximus, who was an orator.

EDCS-05500371.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely a dedication or a name, possibly related to the imperial cult or a person named Venilius Augustus.

EDCS-05500437.translation: Vegetus, son of Marius, is buried here. Sailcius, son of Laucus, had this made at his own expense while alive.
EDCS-05500437.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Vegetus and indicates that Sailcius, alive, commissioned the monument for himself.

EDCS-05500450.translation: Bolosa, daughter of Toutonus, is buried here.
EDCS-05500450.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Bolosa, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500472.text_edition: \n Dea Ataecina Turibrigensis Proserpina per tuam maiestatem te rogo oro obsecro uti vindices quot sic mihi furti factum est quisquis mihi immudavit sic involavit minusve fecit eas res quae infra scripta sunt tunicas VI paenula lintea II indusium cuius m ignoro ius\n
EDCS-05500472.translation: Dea Ataecina Turibrigensis, Proserpina, through your majesty I beg, ask, and implore you to avenge the theft committed against me; whoever changed, stole, or diminished these items which are listed below: six tunics, two wool cloaks, one linen undergarment, whose owner I do not know.
EDCS-05500472.summary: A dedication and prayer to the goddess Ataecina, asking for divine intervention to recover stolen goods, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500494.translation: To Gaius Titius Similus Agripinensis, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, procurator of the province of Lusitania and Vettonia, curator of the public affairs of Emerita, procurator of the province of Moesia Inferior, with the power of the sword, prefect of detachments from the expedition through Asia, Lycia, Pamphylia, and Phrygia, first centurion of the III Augusta Pia Vindex legion, centurion of the frumentarii, centurion of the X legion.
EDCS-05500494.summary: A lengthy cursus honorum for Gaius Titius Similus, detailing his military and administrative career, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500495.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Publius Aelius Vitalis, freedman of the emperor, a scribe for the province of Lusitania and Vettonia. Stephanus, freedman and heir, made this for his patron.
EDCS-05500495.summary: A funerary dedication for Publius Aelius Vitalis, a provincial scribe, erected by his freedman and heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500496.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Julia Optata, aged 23. Publius Aelius Alexander, freedman of the emperor, a scribe for the province of Lusitania, made this for his most sweet wife.
EDCS-05500496.summary: A funerary dedication for Julia Optata, erected by her husband, Publius Aelius Alexander, a provincial scribe, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500497.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Aelia Agrippina, his most rare and most holy wife, who lived 25 years. Hypaticus, freedman of the emperors, sub-procurator, made this for her who deserved well.
EDCS-05500497.summary: A funerary dedication for Aelia Agrippina, erected by her husband Hypaticus, a sub-procurator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500498.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilius Pudentianus, aged 13, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Aemilius Pudens, a soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, made this for his most devoted son.
EDCS-05500498.summary: A funerary inscription for a young boy, Aemilius Pudentianus, erected by his father, a soldier of the VII Gemina Felix legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500500.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Julius Severus, veteran of the VI Victrix legion, aged 61. Iulia Danae, a freedwoman, arranged for this to be made according to the will. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500500.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Severus, a veteran of the VI Victrix legion, whose burial was arranged by his freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500501.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Maelonius Aper, veteran of the VI Victrix Pia Fidelis legion, aged 69. He served as a *beneficiarius consularis*. Lucius Maelonius Primitivus, Maelonia Caesiola, and Maelonia Maelia, a freedwoman, had this made at their own expense for their most devoted patron. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500501.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Maelonius Aper, a veteran of the VI Victrix legion, erected by his freedman and freedwomen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500504.translation: He is buried here. Sacred to the departed spirits. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Gaius Valerius Hymineus of Emerita, a Roman citizen, freedman of Valeria Viniciana, perpetual flaminica. He made this for himself and his wife while alive, and dedicated it. Camilia Chrysampelis, aged 55. Viniciana, aged 35. Hymineus, aged 60.
EDCS-05500504.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Hymineus, his wife, and possibly another relative, with Hymineus being a freedman of a perpetual flaminica, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500505.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iuvinia Sabina, aged 36, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lucius Iuvinius, a member of the VIvirate, [made this].
EDCS-05500505.summary: This is a tombstone for Iuvinia Sabina, erected by Lucius Iuvinius, likely her husband or relative and a member of a six-man college.

EDCS-05500509.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cassius Victorinus, a retiarius (net-fighter), aged 35, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Antonia Severa had this made.
EDCS-05500509.summary: This is a tombstone for Cassius Victorinus, a gladiator, erected by Antonia Severa.

EDCS-05500514.translation: Salvius Pacius Caesar(inus?) lies here, 12 feet in front.
EDCS-05500514.summary: This epitaph records the burial place of Salvius Pacius Caesar(inus?), indicating a burial plot measuring 12 feet across the front.

EDCS-05500519.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Norbana Calephasis, from Emerita, aged 53, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Norbani Polytimus and Capitolina, fellow freedmen, made this.
EDCS-05500519.summary: This tombstone commemorates Norbana Calephasis and was erected by her fellow freedmen, Norbani Polytimus and Capitolina.

EDCS-05500520.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Attius Firminus, from Interamna, aged 52, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Caecilia Eutichia, his most devoted wife, and Attius Augustianus, his freedman, to their excellent patron, made this.
EDCS-05500520.summary: This tombstone honors Marcus Attius Firminus, erected by his wife Caecilia Eutichia and his freedman Attius Augustianus.

EDCS-05500521.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Coronio Celso of Interamna, aged 26. Coronius Rufus and Iulia Rufina had this made for their most devoted son. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500521.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated to Marcus Coronio Celso by his parents, Coronius Rufus and Iulia Rufina.

EDCS-05500522.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Licinius Paternus of Interamna, aged 75. Licinia Paterna for her father and Licinia Flavina for her most excellent husband made this.
EDCS-05500522.summary: This tombstone honors Quintus Licinius Paternus, erected by his daughter Licinia Paterna and his wife Licinia Flavina.

EDCS-05500523.translation: [Several individuals, including?] us, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, aged 58; [and?] ter, of the Papiria tribe, aged 60; Papiria Italica, aged 55. May the earth lie lightly on you all. [Several individuals, including?]nus Daphnus Nico and [one?] from Terentius Rufus made this according to the will.
EDCS-05500523.summary: This monument likely commemorates multiple members of the Papiria tribe, with the final section indicating it was constructed according to a will.

EDCS-05500524.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Attius Natalis, from Lame, aged 18, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lucius Attius Avitianus for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-05500524.summary: This tombstone commemorates Lucius Attius Natalis, erected by his brother Lucius Attius Avitianus.

EDCS-05500530.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Iulius Longinus of the Quirina tribe, from Tapora, aged 35. Lucius Iulius Avitus for his most devoted son.
EDCS-05500530.summary: This monument honors Lucius Iulius Longinus, erected by his father Lucius Iulius Avitus.

EDCS-05500531.translation: Lupus, son of Albinus, aged 50 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500531.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Lupus, son of Albinus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500535.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelia Severa, who lived 10 years, 5 months, and 24 days, made by Aelius Saturninus and Aelia Severa for their most beloved daughter.
EDCS-05500535.summary: A funerary monument set up by parents for their deceased daughter, Aelia Severa, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500539.translation: Publius Alfius, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, made this monument for himself, for Titus Alfius, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe, his brother, and for Alfia Iucunda, his freedwoman, at his own expense.
EDCS-05500539.summary: A tomb monument commissioned by Publius Alfius for himself, his brother, and a freedwoman, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500544.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia Suavola, aged 27 years, [is buried here]. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia Suavola, aged 26 years. Antonia Suavola, the most dutiful mother, arranged for her daughter's burial. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500544.summary: A funerary inscription likely commemorating two individuals named Antonia Suavola, one a mother and the other a daughter, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500546.translation: Asellio Mercurius, aged 40 years. Caecilia Germana made this for her most deserving husband. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500546.summary: A tombstone commemorating Asellio Mercurius, erected by his wife Caecilia Germana, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500550.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [For] Gaius Betutius [---], aged 40 years [---]. Asperinia [---], mother [---]
EDCS-05500550.summary: A partially preserved funerary inscription for Gaius Betutius and possibly his mother Asperinia, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500552.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Cartilia Pantoclia, most beloved infant, 3 years, 4 months, and 2 days [old]. Candidianus and Emerita made this for their most longed-for daughter. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500552.summary: A funerary monument erected by parents for their very young daughter, Cartilia Pantoclia, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500553.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cassia Policarpia, aged 30 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Ianuarius for his deserving wife.
EDCS-05500553.summary: A tombstone for Cassia Policarpia, erected by her husband Ianuarius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500555.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Catulla Mariana, slave, aged 25 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Primitius made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-05500555.summary: A funerary inscription for Catulla Mariana, a slave, erected by Primitius, likely her husband, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500559.translation: Sextus Cornelius Rusticus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500559.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Sextus Cornelius Rusticus, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500563.translation: To the departed spirits. Here lies Egnatia Rufina, aged thirty years. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Caecilius Trophimus had this made for his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-05500563.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Egnatia Rufina, commissioned by her husband Caecilius Trophimus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500564.translation: Egnatia Turpa, daughter of Lucius, aged seventy-five years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500564.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Egnatia Turpa, daughter of Lucius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500565.translation: Fabia Cellaria, aged fourteen years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Cornelius Hilarus and his freedman and foster-son Fabius Suppestes had this made for his chaste wife.
EDCS-05500565.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Fabia Cellaria, commissioned by her husband Cornelius Hilarus and freedman/foster-son Fabius Suppestes, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500566.translation: To the departed spirits. Daughter Bace lived eleven years. Aelia Strena (?) made this for her well-deserving wife. Here she lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500566.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Bace, commissioned by Aelia Strena (?), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500568.translation: Helvia Prima, freedwoman of Marcus, and Helvia, daughter of Erotio, lie here. Nyphas made this.
EDCS-05500568.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of two women, Helvia Prima and Helvia, commissioned by Nyphas, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500571.translation: Marcus Helvius Silo, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, aged two years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. // Gaius Helvius, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe...
EDCS-05500571.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Marcus Helvius Silo, with a damaged and incomplete second entry, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500572.translation: To the departed spirits. Irene, daughter of Valentis, aged fifty years. Fuscianus had this made for his partner. Here she lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500572.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Irene, daughter of Valentis, commissioned by Fuscianus as her contubernalis (partner), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500573.translation: My mother Gaeina raised this tombstone with father Soshenes, lamenting greatly for me. I was seven months old, and my name was Iulianus. // By name Iulianus, it was not permitted to complete seven months; both parents wept greatly.
EDCS-05500573.summary: This bilingual funerary epitaph in Greek and Latin records the premature death of a child named Iulianus, commissioned by his parents, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500574.translation: To the departed spirits. Iunius Hermias had this made for his wife, Iunia Maxima (?), the most excellent and deserving.
EDCS-05500574.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Iunia Maxima, commissioned by her husband Iunius Hermias, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500580.translation: To the departed spirits. Iunia Capella, aged thirty-five years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Iunia Eugenia [made this] for her sister.
EDCS-05500580.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Iunia Capella, commissioned by her sister Iunia Eugenia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500581.translation: Iunia Suavis, aged 21, freedwoman of Iunia Primigenia, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500581.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a freedwoman named Iunia Suavis, identified by her age and her patroness, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500583.translation: Marcus Iunius Sempronianus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, aged 31, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His most dutiful mother arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500583.summary: This tombstone inscription records the burial of Marcus Iunius Sempronianus, including his age, tribal affiliation, and the fact that his mother commissioned the monument, typical of the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500584.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Gaius Lancius Iulianus, aged 16, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Julia Proba, his mother, arranged for this to be made for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-05500584.summary: This funerary epitaph, dedicated to the gods of the underworld, records the death of Gaius Lancius Iulianus at age 16, with his mother commissioning the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500585.translation: Lepecellus, of the Lopilii. Is buried here. 11 feet in front, 7 feet in the field.
EDCS-05500585.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot, specifying its dimensions in front and in depth, belonging to someone named Lepecellus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500587.translation: Gaius Mansuanius Faustus, freedman of Gaius, aged 57. 12 feet in front, 8 feet in the field.
EDCS-05500587.summary: This inscription records the burial of Gaius Mansuanius Faustus, a freedman, including his age and the dimensions of his burial plot, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500588.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marius Drosus annorum LXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Marius Tychius patri karissimo posuit\n
EDCS-05500588.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marius Drosus, aged 70, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Marius Tychius placed this for his dearest father.
EDCS-05500588.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marius Drosus, aged 70, with a sentiment wishing him peace and noting that his son, Marius Tychius, erected the monument, belonging to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500591.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Munatia Emmis, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her most dutiful mother made this for her daughter.
EDCS-05500591.summary: This epitaph is dedicated to the gods of the underworld for Munatia Emmis, aged 25, and was commissioned by her mother, characteristic of Roman Imperial funerary practices.

EDCS-05500592.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Octavius Asius... for his most dutiful wife Octavia Briseis, he made this memorial. She lived 54 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05500592.summary: This epitaph honors Octavia Briseis, the wife of Octavius Asius, noting her age at death and intended as a memorial, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500593.translation: Aeterna Alli, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Procula Marci.
EDCS-05500593.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Aeterna Alli, aged 40, with the name Procula Marci possibly indicating the dedicator or a relative, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500594.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pompeia Quintilla, freedwoman of Marcus, and Marcus Pompeius Charinus are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you both. Marcus Pompeius Firmanus and Pompeia Badia, co-heirs, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05500594.summary: This inscription records the joint burial of Pompeia Quintilla and Marcus Pompeius Charinus, with co-heirs Marcus Pompeius Firmanus and Pompeia Badia commissioning the monument, typical of the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500595.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pompeia Rodope nnorum XL hic sita est viva sibi memoriam fecit\n
EDCS-05500595.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Pompeia Rodope, aged 40, lies here; she made this memorial for herself while alive.
EDCS-05500595.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Pompeia Rodope, who erected her own tombstone.

EDCS-05500597.translation: Sextus Pompeius Aquilus, aged 50; Pompeia Festa, freedwoman of Galates, aged [3]; Pompeia Primigenia, aged 21; lie here; may the earth lie lightly on you. In the land 12 feet, in front 12 and a half feet.
EDCS-05500597.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Pompeius Aquilus and two Pompeia women, likely family members, with details on plot size, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500600.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Publius Pomponius Diophanes, aged 77. Aculeia Bacchis had this made for her well-deserving husband. He lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500600.summary: A funerary epitaph where Aculeia Bacchis commissioned a tomb for her husband, Publius Pomponius Diophanes, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500607.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Statia Nũsa, aged 55. Statia Mercurialis, fellow freedwoman, lie here; may the earth lie lightly on you!
EDCS-05500607.summary: A funerary epitaph for Statia Nũsa, commemorated by Statia Mercurialis, possibly a fellow freedwoman, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500622.text_edition: \n Graecia Modesta heic sic sita est sit tibi terra levis lege et vale locus pedum XII\n
EDCS-05500622.translation: Graecia Modesta lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Read and farewell. The place is 12 feet.
EDCS-05500622.summary: A tombstone for Graecia Modesta, including a brief epitaph and plot dimensions, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500629.text_edition: \n Genio Turgalensium Lucius Crusinus Primigenius votum solvit\n
EDCS-05500629.translation: To the Genius of Turgalensium. Lucius Crusinus Primigenius fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-05500629.summary: A dedication to the Genius of the local community of Turgalensium by Lucius Crusinus Primigenius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500635.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Atia Doquiri filia Severa annorum XVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Q frater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05500635.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Atia Doquiri, daughter of Severa, aged 18, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Her brother Q. had this made.
EDCS-05500635.summary: A funerary epitaph for Atia Doquiri, commissioned by her brother, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500636.text_edition: \n Boudinna Carai filia hic sita est\n
EDCS-05500636.translation: Boudinna, daughter of Carai, lies here.
EDCS-05500636.summary: A simple funerary epitaph recording the burial of Boudinna, daughter of Carai, likely from the Roman provincial period.

EDCS-05500638.text_edition: \n Caesia Cili filia annorum XXV hic sita est mater faciendum curavit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05500638.translation: Caesia, daughter of Cilius, aged 25, lies here. Her mother had this made; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05500638.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caesia, daughter of Cilius, commissioned by her mother, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500641.text_edition: \n Titus Iulius Niger annorum LXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis filii faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-05500641.translation: Titus Iulius Niger, aged 80, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. His sons had this made.
EDCS-05500641.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Iulius Niger, commissioned by his sons, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05500643.text_edition: \n Mailo Arqui filio annorum XX hic situs est\n
EDCS-05500643.translation: Mailo, son of Arqui, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-05500643.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Mailo, son of Arqui, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500669.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caesia Corneliae liberta annorum LXXV hic sita sit est tibi terra levis filii et filiae faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-05500669.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caesia Cornelia, freedwoman, aged 75, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her children had this made.
EDCS-05500669.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesia Cornelia, a freedwoman, commissioned by her children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500675.translation: Germanus, slave, aged 21, is buried here. [Belonging to] Nero[n] of Germanicus Cla[udius Cae]sar.
EDCS-05500675.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Germanus, a slave of the imperial family (likely Nero), dating to the 1st century AD.

EDCS-05500676.text_edition: \n Caia Iulia Bovana Triti filia hic sita est\n
EDCS-05500676.translation: Caia Iulia Bovana, daughter of Triti, is buried here.
EDCS-05500676.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Caia Iulia Bovana, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500684.translation: Tritius, son of Tanginus, is buried here. Amoena [made this].
