EDCS-08600347.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Euhodia, a slave of Mellinus, aged 15, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Euhodus and Callityche [did this for] their most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-08600347.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Euhodia, a slave of Mellinus, aged 15, erected by her parents Euhodus and Callityche.

EDCS-08600348.text_edition: \n Caesilia Titi filia Anulla annorum LV \n
EDCS-08600348.translation: Caesilia Anulla, daughter of Titus, aged 55.
EDCS-08600348.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Caesilia Anulla, daughter of Titus.

EDCS-08600349.translation: To Calpurnius Capito, son of Capito, aged 60.
EDCS-08600349.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Calpurnius Capito, son of Capito.

EDCS-08900422.text_edition: \n Marcus Furius Florus militavit annis Italicus natione vixit ann\n
EDCS-08900422.translation: Marcus Furius Florus, served for [some number of] years, of Italian origin, lived [some number of] years.
EDCS-08900422.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Marcus Furius Florus, noting his military service and origin.

EDCS-08900426.summary: This is a funerary or property inscription for Lucius Gallus Fronto, dating to an uncertain period.

EDCS-08900429.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Gaio Iulio Vero Maximino Pio Felici Augusto\n
EDCS-08900429.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-08900429.summary: This inscription is an acclamation or dedication to Emperor Maximinus Thrax, dating to his reign (235-238 AD).

EDCS-08901000.text_edition: \n Caturoni Valuti filio nnorum LXX Tureus Maducei filius ex testamento eius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08901000.translation: Tureus, son of Maduceius, had this made in accordance with his will for Caturon, son of Valutus, aged 70.
EDCS-08901000.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Tureus for Caturon, who died at the age of 70, with the work being carried out as per Caturon's will.

EDCS-08901001.text_edition: \n Caburenae Celi annorum IIX Tongea Pentobi \n
EDCS-08901001.translation: Caburena, daughter of Celius, aged 8, [and] Tongea, daughter of Pentobius.
EDCS-08901001.summary: This inscription likely commemorates two young girls, Caburena (8 years old) and Tongea, possibly in a funerary context.

EDCS-08901002.text_edition: \n Inter Coilarnos Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VI patri patriae consuli III terminus Augustalis Inter Raba\n
EDCS-08901002.translation: To Antoninus Pius, the great and powerful god, pontiff, with tribunician power for the sixth time, father of the fatherland, consul for the third time. To Jupiter, protector of the Augustan road...
EDCS-08901002.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely associated with a road or boundary marker ('terminus Augustalis'), dated to his sixth tribunician power and third consulship.

EDCS-08901004.text_edition: \n Gaius Flavius Luci filius Galeria Toncius hic situs est\n
EDCS-08901004.translation: Gaius Flavius Toncius, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, lies here.
EDCS-08901004.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Flavius Toncius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-09001025.text_edition: \n Terentia Luci filia Tertulla Sempronia Marci filia Crispina filia nnorum XL hic sitae sunt\n
EDCS-09001025.translation: Terentia, daughter of Lucius, and Tertulla, daughter of Marcus Crispina, aged 40, lie here.
EDCS-09001025.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two women, Terentia and Tertulla, with Tertulla being 40 years old.

EDCS-09001027.text_edition: \n Valeria Maura Macri liberta annorum LXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Crescens filius de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09001027.translation: Valeria Maura, freedwoman of Macrus, aged 75, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Her son Crescens had this made at his own expense.
EDCS-09001027.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Maura, a freedwoman, erected by her son Crescens.

EDCS-09200308.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Iulius Rufinus Gai Iuli Rufi filius votum solvit animo libens\n
EDCS-09200308.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Gaius Julius Rufinus, son of Gaius Julius Rufus, has paid his vow willingly.
EDCS-09200308.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Gaius Julius Rufinus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09200311.text_edition: \n Bellonae Galitigius Augustianus fecit ex voto\n
EDCS-09200311.translation: Galitigius Augustianus made this for Bellona, according to his vow.
EDCS-09200311.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Bellona by Galitigius Augustianus.

EDCS-09300262.text_edition: \n Miniciae Paternae nnorum XXXV coniugi dulcissimae et castissimae Geminius Gargilianus miles legionis VII geminae beneficiarius\n
EDCS-09300262.translation: To Minicia Paternia, wife of 35 years, most sweet and most chaste, Geminius Gargilianus, a soldier of the Seventh Gemina Legion, a beneficiary.
EDCS-09300262.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Minicia Paternia, set up by her husband, a soldier of the Seventh Legion.

EDCS-09300263.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Annio Romano militi legionis VII geminae piae felicis nnorum XXXXIII stipendiorum XXVI mensium VIII Arria Hispanilla mater filio piissimo fecit\n
EDCS-09300263.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Annio Romanus, soldier of the Seventh Gemina Pious and Fortunate Legion, aged 43 years, 26 years of service, 8 months. Arria Hispanilla, his mother, had this made for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-09300263.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annio Romanus, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, erected by his mother.

EDCS-09300963.text_edition: \n Cabrulae Prisci filiae matri Prisca Reburri filia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09300963.translation: Prisca, daughter of Reburrus, had this made for her mother Cabrula, daughter of Priscus.
EDCS-09300963.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Prisca for her mother, Cabrula.

EDCS-09300982.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Cornelius.
EDCS-09300982.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription for someone named Cornelius.

EDCS-09300999.text_edition: \n Aucalus Appini filius hic situs est annorum XX et Abrunus Arcelti filius Appin\n
EDCS-09300999.translation: Aucalus, son of Appinus, is laid here, aged 20 years, and Abrunus, son of Arceltus, son of Appinus.
EDCS-09300999.summary: This is a joint funerary inscription for Aucalus and Abrunus, sons of Appinus, recording their ages.

EDCS-09301000.text_edition: \n Peinuga Aueli filia hic sita est\n
EDCS-09301000.translation: Peinuga, daughter of Avelus, is laid here.
EDCS-09301000.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Peinuga, daughter of Avelus.

EDCS-09301002.text_edition: \n ------ Am]bati filia annorum XXXI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Gaius Cercenna vir et pater faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09301002.translation: [---] daughter of Amba, aged 31 years, is laid here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Gaius Cercenna, her husband and father, had this made.
EDCS-09301002.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of a 31-year-old woman, commissioned by her husband and father.

EDCS-09301017.text_edition: \n Iulius Coutius Alboni filius Iovi Repultsorio sic libens animo votum solvit\n
EDCS-09301017.translation: Julius Coutius, son of Albonius, willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Repulsor.
EDCS-09301017.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Julius Coutius fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Repulsor.

EDCS-09400456.text_edition: \n Quadrato Mantai filio Turdulo veteri Celsus Celeris filius ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09400456.translation: Celsus, son of Celeris, had this made in accordance with the will of Quadratus, son of Mantus, the elder Turdulus.
EDCS-09400456.summary: This monument was erected according to a will for Quadratus, son of Mantus, also known as Turdulus the elder.

EDCS-09400457.translation: Amoena, daughter of Cilius, aged [---] Catur[a ---].
EDCS-09400457.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary epitaph for Amoena, daughter of Cilius, recording her age and possibly her origin.

EDCS-09400459.text_edition: \n Amoeneae Cacalonis filiae Lucius Talabi filius uxsori sic\n
EDCS-09400459.translation: To Amoenea, daughter of Cacalo. Lucius, son of Talabius, [dedicated this] to his wife.
EDCS-09400459.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the late Republic or early Empire, is dedicated by Lucius Talabius to his wife, Amoenea.

EDCS-09700170.text_edition: \n Messius Luci filius Galeria Rebilus hic situs est\n
EDCS-09700170.translation: Messius Rebilus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-09700170.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Messius Rebilus, son of Lucius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700177.text_edition: \n Accius Amoeni liberti libertus annorum XXC\n
EDCS-09700177.translation: Accius, freedman of Amoenus' freedman, aged 80.
EDCS-09700177.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age and former status of Accius, a freedman of a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700178.text_edition: \n Ambatus pius in suis annorum XXXXV\n
EDCS-09700178.translation: Ambatus, dutiful to his own people, aged 45.
EDCS-09700178.summary: This epitaph records the age and familial piety of Ambatus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700180.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marco Ussio Situano veterano legionis VII geminae vixit annos Val[erius ------\n
EDCS-09700180.translation: To the Divine Shades. Valerius [dedicated this] to Marcus Ussius Situanus, a veteran of Legio VII Gemina, who lived [X] years.
EDCS-09700180.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Ussius Situanus, a veteran of Legio VII Gemina, with an incomplete record of his lifespan, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700591.text_edition: \n Accius Bouti filius Aloncus Instiniensis hic situs est Taurus Acci filius Ammicius statuit et scripsit Bolosa Vapi filia\n
EDCS-09700591.translation: Accius Aloncus, son of Boutus, of Instiniensis, is buried here. Taurus Ammicius, son of Accius, erected and inscribed [this], and Bolosa, daughter of Vapi.
EDCS-09700591.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Accius Aloncus and its erection by Taurus Ammicius and Bolosa Vapi, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700593.text_edition: \n Aervinae divinae Varia Clementis filia votum solvit\n
EDCS-09700593.translation: Varia Clementis, daughter of [dedicated this] to Aervina the Divine, fulfilling her vow.
EDCS-09700593.summary: This votive inscription records Varia Clementis fulfilling a vow to the goddess Aervina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700597.text_edition: \n Caino Caturoni filius hic situs sit tibi terra levis S\n
EDCS-09700597.translation: Cainus, son of Caturon, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09700597.summary: This simple funerary inscription records the burial of Cainus, son of Caturon, with a wish for his peaceful repose, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700598.translation: Pellius, son of Boutus. To the Departed Shades.
EDCS-09700598.summary: This very brief inscription, possibly funerary, names Pellius, son of Boutus, and dedicates it to the departed spirits, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700600.text_edition: \n Quintus Verrucius Gemellus Quintus Verrucius Urbicus\n
EDCS-09700600.translation: Quintus Verrucius Gemellus [and] Quintus Verrucius Urbicus.
EDCS-09700600.summary: This inscription simply lists two names, Quintus Verrucius Gemellus and Quintus Verrucius Urbicus, likely commemorating individuals or a dedication, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700610.text_edition: \n Cornelia ONE filia hic sita est\n
EDCS-09700610.translation: Cornelia, daughter of ONE, is buried here.
EDCS-09700610.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Cornelia, daughter of ONE, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700974.text_edition: \n Ammiae Pistiricum Pistiri filiae Aegandus et Amaenea matri faciendum curaverunt Proculae Pentaniqum Bouti filiae Aegandus uxsori faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09700974.translation: Aegandus and Amaenea arranged for this to be made for their mother, Ammia Pistiricum, daughter of Pistirus. Aegandus arranged for this to be made for his wife, Procula, daughter of Pentaniqum and granddaughter of Boutis.
EDCS-09700974.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Aegandus and Amaenea for their mother Ammia Pistiricum, and also by Aegandus for his wife Procula.

EDCS-09701492.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Attices annorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Eros uxori pientissimae ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-09701492.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Attices, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Eros arranged for this to be placed for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-09701492.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Attices, aged 25, and was set up by her husband Eros.

EDCS-09701503.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Norbana Maura.

EDCS-09800107.text_edition: \n Bassus Allucqui filius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09800107.translation: Bassus, son of Allucqus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09800107.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Bassus, son of Allucqus, with a common wish for a light grave.

EDCS-09800109.text_edition: \n Caeno Lovesi filius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09800109.translation: Caeno, son of Lovesus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09800109.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Caeno, son of Lovesus, with a common wish for a light grave.

EDCS-09800113.text_edition: \n Qasae sic Tarantelli Amoena filia Maela Severi filia Tanginus Arantoni filius Turantius Lovesi filius faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09800113.translation: Qasa, daughter of Tarantellus; Amoena, daughter of Maela; Severus, son of Tanginus; and Turantius, son of Lovesus, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09800113.summary: This inscription records that Qasa, Amoena, Severus, and Turantius jointly commissioned this monument.

EDCS-09800123.text_edition: \n Flacco Tongi filio et Avitae Tongi filiae Cilura Tongi filio et filiae Gracilis Vari filius matri et avunculo faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09800123.translation: Varius, son of Gracchus, arranged for this to be made for his mother Cilura and his sister Gracilis, daughter of Tongs; and for Flaccus, son of Tongs, and Avita, daughter of Tongs.
EDCS-09800123.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by Varius for his mother, sister, and two other individuals named Flaccus and Avita, likely relatives.

EDCS-09800140.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Vegeto Vegetini filio Interamniensi Amoena Nigri liberta heres marito et sibi faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09800140.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Amoena, freedwoman of Nigrus, arranged for this to be made for her husband Vegetus, son of Vegetinus, from Interamna, and for herself.
EDCS-09800140.summary: This funerary inscription records that Amoena, a freedwoman, commissioned this monument for her husband Vegetus and for herself.

EDCS-09800141.text_edition: \n Ab Emerita CXX ad Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus cons(ul) XIII imperator XVI\n
EDCS-09800141.translation: From Emerita, 120 [miles] to Imperator Caesar, son of the Divine Augustus, consul for the 13th time, imperator for the 16th time.
EDCS-09800141.summary: This is a milestone marking a distance of 120 miles from Emerita, dedicated to Emperor Augustus during his consulship and reign.

EDCS-09800143.text_edition: \n Caius Pompeius Luci filius Papiria Priscus flamen coloniae IIvir flamen provinciae Lusitaniae huic ordo decrevit locum sepulturae impensam funeris statuam et laudationem nnorum XXXVII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09800143.translation: To Gaius Pompeius Priscus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, priest of the colony, duumvir, priest of the province of Lusitania. The town council decreed for him a burial place, funeral expenses, a statue, and funeral orations. He is 37 years old and lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09800143.summary: This epitaph records the honors granted to Gaius Pompeius Priscus by his town council, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09800147.text_edition: \n Iulia primarum castissima haec feminarum Sotira dulcis amor hoc tegitur tumulo quot potuit pietas plus meritae fecit quisque legis dicas sit tibi terra levis Iulia Sotira annorum XXIII hic sita est Liberinus maritus et Iulia Liberina filia fecerunt\n
EDCS-09800147.translation: Iulia Sotira, sweetest love, the most chaste of women of the first rank, lies in this tomb; whoever could, out of devotion, did more for her who deserved it. Whoever reads, say: May the earth lie lightly on you. Iulia Sotira, 23 years old, lies here. Her husband Liberinus and daughter Iulia Liberina made this.
EDCS-09800147.summary: This epitaph is for Iulia Sotira, erected by her husband and daughter, and dates to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09800150.text_edition: \n Augustae Trebarune Marcus Fidius Fidi filius Quirina Macer magistratus III IIvir II praefectus fabrum\n
EDCS-09800150.translation: To Marcus Macer, son of Fidus, of the Quirina tribe, magistrate, duumvir, praefectus fabrum, of the colony Augusta Trebaruna.
EDCS-09800150.summary: This dedication honors Marcus Macer, a local official in Augusta Trebaruna, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102234.text_edition: \n Victoriae Caius Iulius Iulianus animo libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-10102234.translation: Gaius Iulius Iulianus willingly fulfilled his vow to Victoria.
EDCS-10102234.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Gaius Iulius Iulianus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Victoria, probably from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10701760.text_edition: \n Lucio Pomponio Marci filio Capitoni IIviro coloniae Augustae Emeritae praefecto fabrum flamini coloniae Augustae Emeritae flamini provinciae Lusitaniae divi Augusti divae Augustae Aulo Vitellio Lucio f(ilio) Vipstano consulibus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-10701760.translation: By decree of the decurions, to Lucius Pomponius Capito, son of Marcus, duumvir of the colony Augusta Emerita, prefect of engineers, priest of the colony Augusta Emerita, priest of the province of Lusitania, under the consulship of Divus Augustus, Diva Augusta, and Aulus Vitellius, son of Lucius Vipstanus.
EDCS-10701760.summary: This dedication honors Lucius Pomponius Capito, a prominent local magistrate and priest in Augusta Emerita, during the reign of Augustus and Tiberius.

EDCS-10701763.translation: Saturninus, son of M. Cilo, aged 30.
EDCS-10701763.summary: This is a simple epitaph recording the name, parentage, and age of Saturninus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11701025.summary: This inscription likely lists names, possibly of a family or household, with the name Hestas appearing above three others, probably from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11701028.summary: This is an ownership mark from the workshop of a craftsman named Dexter, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11701080.text_edition: \n Caius Calpurnius nus et Gaius Vibius Quintilianus et Lucius Attius us et Marcus Verrius <G>eminus solum tessellasque de suo straverunt et donarunt\n
EDCS-11701080.translation: Gaius Calpurnius [Nus?], Gaius Vibius Quintilianus, Lucius Attius [us?], and Marcus Verrius Geminus paved and donated the floor and tiles from their own resources.
EDCS-11701080.summary: This inscription records the joint donation of a tessellated floor by four individuals, likely a public benefaction, probably from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11800780.text_edition: \n Have Passeria Luci filia Romula annorum IIII hic sita est\n
EDCS-11800780.translation: Greetings! Passeria Romula, daughter of Lucius, aged 4, lies here.
EDCS-11800780.summary: This epitaph records the death of a young girl, Passeria Romula, and is likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11800862.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, from the community of the Cobelci.
EDCS-11800862.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by the community of the Cobelci.

EDCS-11800863.text_edition: \n Capito Medami filius miles cohortis primae Inturaiorum Iovi votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-11800863.translation: Capito, son of Medamus, a soldier of the first cohort of the Inturaii, willingly paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-11800863.summary: This inscription records a soldier of the Inturaii cohort fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11800865.text_edition: \n Dis Leia dedit Pabanicus votum solvit\n
EDCS-11800865.translation: Leia gave to the gods; Pabanicus paid the vow.
EDCS-11800865.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the gods by Leia and Pabanicus.

EDCS-11800869.text_edition: \n Lucio Antonio Luci filio Quirina Vegeto Turdulo Iunia Gai filia Serena mater filio piissimo fecit\n
EDCS-11800869.translation: Junia Serena, mother, made this for her most dutiful son, Lucius Antonius Vegetus Turdulus, son of Lucius Antonius, of the tribe Quirina.
EDCS-11800869.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument set up by a mother for her son, Lucius Antonius Vegetus Turdulus.

EDCS-11800871.translation: Lucius Norbanus Pius, grandson of Lucius.
EDCS-11800871.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple dedication or honorary title for Lucius Norbanus Pius.

EDCS-11800872.text_edition: \n Lucius Norbanus Cali filius Calus annorum LXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-11800872.translation: Lucius Norbanus Calus, son of Calus, is buried here, seventy-five years old; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11800872.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Norbanus Calus, dating to the Roman Imperial period, including a standard wish for the deceased.

EDCS-11800890.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Felix nnorum XXXIII Memmius Fortunatus filio piissumo sic fecit hic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11800890.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Felix, aged thirty-three years. Memmius Fortunatus made this for his most dutiful son; may he lie here, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11800890.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Memmius Fortunatus for his deceased son Felix, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901230.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods. Lucius Agrius Vocatus, priest, made a cincture offering.
EDCS-11901230.summary: This inscription records a priestly dedication to the Magna Mater by Lucius Agrius Vocatus.

EDCS-11901232.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Domitio Aureliano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate patri patriae II consuli proconsuli res publica Ossonobensium ex decreto ordinis devota numini maiestatique eius dedit dedicavitque\n
EDCS-11901232.translation: The Republic of the Ossonobensians, by decree of the council, devoted and dedicated this to the divine majesty of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, father of the fatherland, twice consul, proconsul.
EDCS-11901232.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the Ossonobensian community to Emperor Aurelian, likely dating to his reign (270-275 AD).

EDCS-11901269.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Salianus lived thirty-three years and two months. His father Romulensis placed this for his most dutiful son; may he lie at Barbe(!)
EDCS-11901269.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Salianus, set up by his father Romulensis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901307.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely a marker or a brief statement of ownership, belonging to Coilicus.

EDCS-11901350.text_edition: \n L(ucio) Iunio Luci filio Galeria Philoni IIviro ex decreto decurionum flamini divi Augusti perpetuo Duronius filius Galeria Modestus ndum merit(um) in rem publicam et plebem suam decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-11901350.translation: To Lucius Junius Philon, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, twice duovir, perpetual flamen of the deified Augustus, his son Duronius Modestus, of the Galeria tribe, [dedicated this] by decree of the decurions for his deserving service to the state and his people.
EDCS-11901350.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Junius Philon, a prominent local official, with a dedication by his son, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11901369.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Liberius nnum V hic situs est terra levis\n
EDCS-11901369.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Liberius is buried here. May the earth be light upon him.
EDCS-11901369.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Liberius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901372.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Mersinae nnorum VIIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11901372.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Mersina, aged nine, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-11901372.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Mersina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901393.text_edition: \n Decimo Iulio Decimi filio Galeria Saturnino publici liberti\n
EDCS-11901393.translation: To Decimus Julius Saturninus, son of Decimus, of the Galeria tribe, freedman of a public official.
EDCS-11901393.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Decimus Julius Saturninus, a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901425.text_edition: \n Matri Deum sacrum duo Irinaei pater et filius criobolati natali suo sacerdotibus Lucio Antistio Avito Caio Antistio Felicissimo\n
EDCS-11901425.translation: Sacred to the Mother of the Gods. Two Irinaei, father and son, who underwent the ram's sacrifice, on their birthday, for the priests Lucius Antistius Avitus and Gaius Antistius Felicissimus.
EDCS-11901425.summary: This inscription records a religious dedication by two individuals named Irinaeus to priests of the cult of the Mother of the Gods, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901462.text_edition: \n S(oli) deo Invicto sodalicium Bracarorum studium sua inpensa fecerunt cum cratera titulum donavit Messius libertus? Artemidorus magister de suo fecit\n
EDCS-11901462.translation: To the Only Unconquered God. The association of Bracarenses made this at their own expense with a bowl; Messius, perhaps a freedman, the master, donated the inscription at his own expense.
EDCS-11901462.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Sol Invictus by an association of Bracarenses and a donation of an inscription by Messius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901469.text_edition: \n Hispallus Bocchi servus nnum III hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Euhodus fecit\n
EDCS-11901469.translation: Hispallus, slave of Bocchus, aged three, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you. Euhodus made this.
EDCS-11901469.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hispallus, a young slave, with the monument erected by Euhodus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901487.text_edition: \n Lucius Iulius Philomusus Lucius Iulius Fro[nto ------\n
EDCS-11901487.translation: Lucius Julius Philomusus [and] Lucius Julius Fronto [erected this].
EDCS-11901487.summary: This is an incomplete funerary or commemorative inscription for Lucius Julius Philomusus and Lucius Julius Fronto, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901497.text_edition: \n Quintus Tullius Habiti filius Galeria Modestus annorum XX Tullia Habiti filia Tusca annorum V Quintus Alfius Modestus hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-11901497.translation: Quintus Tullius Modestus, son of Habitus, of the Galeria tribe, aged twenty; Tullia Tusca, daughter of Habitus, aged five; Quintus Alfius Modestus – they are buried here. May the earth be light upon you. Their mother took care of the construction.
EDCS-11901497.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of three individuals, including two children, with the monument arranged by their mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11901498.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Vivennia Badia nnorum LXXX Allia Nymphe filia matri pientissimae faciendum curavit hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11901498.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Vivennia Badia, aged 80 years, had this monument prepared by her daughter Allia Nymphe for her most devoted mother. She is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-11901498.summary: This funerary inscription records the preparation of a tomb for Vivennia Badia by her daughter Allia Nymphe, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901504.text_edition: \n Cassius Rufus Tapori filius annorum XXXV CIFCXCE hic situs est \n
EDCS-11901504.translation: Cassius Rufus, son of Tapori, aged 35 years, is buried here.
EDCS-11901504.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Cassius Rufus, son of Tapori, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901508.summary: This brief funerary inscription records the death of Julian at the age of 30, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901530.text_edition: \n Mani Mani filia Camira filiae Iuliae Naurae nnorum XXX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11901530.translation: Mani, daughter of Mani, Camira, had this monument prepared by her daughter for Julia Naura, aged 30 years. She is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-11901530.summary: This funerary inscription records the preparation of a tomb for Julia Naura by her mother Camira, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901531.text_edition: \n Lucius Marius Luci filius Papiria Caprarius hic situs est\n
EDCS-11901531.translation: Lucius Marius, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-11901531.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Marius, son of Lucius, indicating his tribal affiliation, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901535.text_edition: \n Gaius Valerius Louesius hic situs est annorum XXX\n
EDCS-11901535.translation: Gaius Valerius Louesius is buried here, aged 30 years.
EDCS-11901535.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Valerius Louesius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901559.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the name Gaius Julius and his father Caturonis, likely a funerary or commemorative text from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-11901569.text_edition: \n Deo Endovellico Tula ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-11901569.translation: To the god Endovellicus, Tula dedicated this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11901569.summary: This votive inscription records a dedication to the god Endovellicus by Tula, likely from the Roman period in the Iberian Peninsula.

EDCS-11901611.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Preccius Quirina Paetus nnrum LXXV Preccia Vegeti filia Maxuma annorum XXX Preccia Paeti filia Paetilla annorum XII Preccia Arconis filia Tusca annorum XXXV hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-11901611.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Preccius, aged 75, his wife Preccia Maxuma, daughter of Vegetius, aged 30, and their daughter Preccia Paetilla, daughter of Paetus, aged 12, and Preccia Tusca, daughter of Arconis, aged 35, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11901611.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of four individuals, Marcus Preccius and three women, likely his wife and daughters, with their ages and parentage.

EDCS-11901623.text_edition: \n Iovi sacrum Faustus Bassi libertus animo libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-11901623.translation: Sacred to Jupiter. Faustus, freedman of Bassus, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11901623.summary: This votive inscription records Faustus, a freedman, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-11901624.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Fusca Vituli liberta animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-11901624.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Fusca, freedwoman of Vitulus, willingly fulfilled [her vow].
EDCS-11901624.summary: This votive inscription records Fusca, a freedwoman, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-11901625.text_edition: \n Ocrimirae sacrum Iulia Saturisca animo libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-11901625.translation: Sacred to Ocrimira. Iulia Saturisca willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11901625.summary: This votive inscription records Iulia Saturisca fulfilling a vow to the deity Ocrimira.

EDCS-11901626.text_edition: \n Togae Almae sacrum Novella Anniae liberta votum animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-11901626.translation: Sacred to Togae Almae. Novella, freedwoman of Annia, willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11901626.summary: This votive inscription records Novella, a freedwoman, fulfilling a vow to Togae Almae.

EDCS-11901630.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico imperatori III pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate IIII consuli III designato IIII civitas Ammaiensis ex voto annuo Lucio Calventio Vetere Carminio legato Tiberi Claudii Caesaris Augusti Proculo Pisiri filio Omuncione Cilai\n
EDCS-11901630.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, Imperator for the third time, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the fourth time, consul for the third time, designated consul for the fourth time, the community of Ammaiensis, according to its annual vow, [dedicated this] to Lucius Calventius Vetus Carminius, legate of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Proculus, son of Pisirius Omuncion, son of Cilai.
EDCS-11901630.summary: This dedication records a city's vow fulfilled to the Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, mentioning local officials, likely from the 1st century AD.

EDCS-11901667.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Claudio Tacito Pio Felici Invicto Augusto patri patriae II consuli\n
EDCS-11901667.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Claudius Tacitus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, father of the fatherland, consul for the second time.
EDCS-11901667.summary: This dedicatory inscription honors Emperor Tacitus, mentioning his titles and consulship, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11901668.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Probus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, [consul?].
EDCS-11901668.summary: This fragment of a dedicatory inscription honors Emperor Probus, listing some of his titles, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11901672.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Iulio Flavio nobilissimo Caesari XVIIII\n
EDCS-11901672.translation: To our lord Iulius Flavius, most noble Caesar, the nineteenth [year?].
EDCS-11901672.summary: This inscription, likely dated to the late 3rd or early 4th century AD, addresses the Caesar Iulius Flavius.

EDCS-12700140.text_edition: \n Florentissimo ac beatissimo saeculo favente felicitate et clementia dominorum imperatorumque nostrorum Flavi Claudi Constantini maximi victoris et Flavi Iuli Constanti et Flavi Iuli Constantis victorum fortissimorumque semper Augustorum circum vetustate conlapsum Tiberius Flavius Laetus vir clarissimus comes columnis erigi novis ornamentorum fabricis cingi aquis inundari disposuit adque sic ita insistente viro perfectissimo Iulio Saturnino praeside provinciae Lusitaniae ita conpetenter restituta eius facies sic splendidissimae coloniae Emeritensium quam maximam tribuit voluptatem\n
EDCS-12700140.translation: In a most flourishing and blessed era, favored by the fortune and clemency of our lords and emperors, Flavius Claudius Constantinus Victor, Flavius Iulius Constantius, and Flavius Iulius Constans, most valiant victors and ever Augustuses, Tiberius Flavius Laetus, a most distinguished man and comes, arranged for the colonnade, having collapsed with age, to be rebuilt with new structures for adornments and to be flooded with water, and thus, with this most perfect man, Iulius Saturninus, president of the province of Lusitania, presiding, its face was so competently restored that it brought the greatest pleasure to the most splendid colony of Emerita.
EDCS-12700140.summary: This inscription records the restoration of public buildings and water features in the city of Emerita (Mérida) by Tiberius Flavius Laetus, with the support of the provincial governor Iulius Saturninus, under the reign of Emperors Constantinus, Constantius, and Constans, likely in the mid-4th century AD.

EDCS-12700164.text_edition: \n Quintus Acutius Faienanus legatus pro praetore\n
EDCS-12700164.summary: This inscription records the name and title of Quintus Acutius Faienanus, a propraetorian legate, likely on a public monument. The date is inferable from the office held, likely 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-13302638.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lutatia Lupata nnorum XVI Lutatia Severa alumnae hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-13302638.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lutatia Lupata, aged 16, Lutatia Severa, her nurse, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-13302638.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lutatia Lupata, aged 16, set up by her nurse Lutatia Severa. The text dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400162.text_edition: \n Deae Invictae Caelesti Nemesi Marcus Aurelius Fili Roma votum solvit animo libens sacra VS\n
EDCS-13400162.translation: To the Invincible Goddess Caelestis Nemesis, Marcus Aurelius, son of Roma, willingly fulfilled his vow and offered sacred rites.
EDCS-13400162.summary: A dedication to the goddess Caelestis Nemesis by Marcus Aurelius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13400259.text_edition: \n Quintus Iallius Sexti filius Papiria Augurinus orarium donavit Igaeditanis libens animo fecit per magisterium Toutoni Arci filii Malgeini Manli filii Celti Arantoni filii Ammini Ati filii Lucio Domitio Ahenobarbo Publio Cornelio Scipione consulibus\n
EDCS-13400259.translation: Quintus Iallius, son of Sextus, of the Papiria tribe, Augurinus, gladly donated the orarium to the people of Igaeditani, and had it made during the magistracy of Touotis, son of Arcius, Malgeinus, son of Manlius, Celtus, son of Aranton, Amminus, son of Atius, under the consulship of Lucius Domitius Ahenobarbus and Publius Cornelius Scipio.
EDCS-13400259.summary: A donation of an orarium (a cloth worn by officials) by Quintus Iallius to the Igaeditani, with extensive filiation and consuls, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-13600284.text_edition: \n Caneas Canovi filius Iovi Solutori Optimo votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-13600284.translation: Caneas, son of Canovius, willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Solutor Optimo.
EDCS-13600284.summary: A votive offering to Jupiter by Caneas, son of Canovius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13700436.text_edition: \n Lucius Lavius Luci filius Aemilia tribu Tuscus Felicitis sic Iulia miles legionis X geminae victricis ]\n
EDCS-13700436.translation: Lucius Lavius, son of Lucius, of the Aemilia tribe, from Tuscus Felicitis, soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina Victrix...
EDCS-13700436.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Lucius Lavius, a soldier of the Tenth Legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13800097.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consule II civitas Aravorum\n
EDCS-13800097.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the third time, consul for the second time, the community of the Aravi...
EDCS-13800097.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Hadrian, likely marking a public building or monument, dating to his third tribunician power and second consulship (AD 119-121).

EDCS-13900379.text_edition: \n Marco Aemilio Lepido Lucio Arruntio consulibus decuriones et municipes Martienses qui antea Ugienses fuerunt hospitium fecerunt cum decurionibus et colonis coloniae Augustae Emeritae sibi liberis posterisque eorum egerunt legati Publius Mummius Publi filius Galeria Ursus Marcus Aemilius Marci filius Galeria Fronto\n
EDCS-13900379.translation: During the consulship of Marcus Aemilius Lepidus and Lucius Arruntius, the councilors and citizens of Martenses, who were formerly Ugienses, established a treaty of hospitality with the councilors and colonists of the colony of Augusta Emerita, for themselves and their children and descendants. The envoys were Publius Mummius, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe Ursus, and Marcus Aemilius, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe Fronto.
EDCS-13900379.summary: A treaty of hospitality established between the Martenses and the colony of Augusta Emerita, dating to the consulship of Lepidus and Arruntius (AD 6).

EDCS-13900406.text_edition: \n Avitianus decurio civitatis Aravorum annorum XXXXVIII Petra <e=C>ius uxor testamento titulum posuit \n
EDCS-13900406.translation: Avitianus, councilor of the community of the Aravi, aged 48. Petraeus, his wife, placed this inscription by her will.
EDCS-13900406.summary: A funerary inscription for Avitianus, a councilor of the Aravi, placed by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900410.text_edition: \n C is Aeliano o Rufo Iuliano consuli proconsuli provinciae Africae IIVviro sic epulonum fetiali sodali Flaviali Titiali ex testamento Titi Aemili Titi filii Papiria Saturnini\n
EDCS-13900410.translation: To C(aius) [Aeliano]o Rufo Iuliano, consul, proconsul of the province of Africa, IIvir (of the vigintiviri) of the epulones, fetialis priest, Flavial sodalis, Titial, by the will of Titus Aemilius, son of Titus, of the Papiria tribe Saturninus.
EDCS-13900410.summary: An inscription honoring C. Aelianus Rufus Iulianus, detailing his various prestigious offices, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900411.text_edition: \n Sexto Furnio Iuliano legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Lusitaniae clarissimo viro consuli ordo splendidissimus Emeritensium praesidi innocentissimo patrono optimo ob merita io Modest[o ------\n
EDCS-13900411.translation: To Sextus Furnius Iulianus, legate of the emperor with the power of a praetor in the province of Lusitania, most illustrious man, consul, the most splendid order of the Emeritensians, protector, most innocent patron, best, for his merits to Modestus...
EDCS-13900411.summary: A dedication to Sextus Furnius Iulianus, a distinguished imperial legate and former consul, by the citizens of Emerita, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900412.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Antestio Persico Papiri<a=I> Emeritensis nnorum XXXVII IIvirali pontifici perpetuo Iulia Persilla et Antestius Avitianus patri piissimo fecerunt hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-13900412.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. For Lucius Antestius Persicus, an Emeritan of the Papiria tribe, aged 37, perpetual IIvir and pontiff. Iulia Persilla and Antestius Avitianus made this for their most pious father. Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-13900412.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Antestius Persicus, a local magistrate in Emerita, set up by his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14700008.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for someone named Iulius Fuscus, with missing details.

EDCS-14700014.text_edition: \n Andamus Arconis situs hoc marmore miles annorum V M XXX cum bona fama sua sanctusque fuit MISE nomine A aeque merenti ntina pia norus\n
EDCS-14700014.translation: Andamus, son of Arcon, a soldier, aged 35 years, lies here in this marble, and he was holy and deserving with his good reputation. Mise, deserving alike, his wife, [ordered this to be made].
EDCS-14700014.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Andamus, a soldier aged 35, who is commemorated by his wife Mise.

EDCS-14700018.text_edition: \n Vordio Talaconio Marcus Cornelius O qui libens fieri iussit\n
EDCS-14700018.translation: Marcus Cornelius O ordered this to be made willingly for Vordius Talaconius.
EDCS-14700018.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Cornelius O having a monument made for Vordius Talaconius.

EDCS-14700019.text_edition: \n Tangino Talavi filio annorum LXX patri et Tangino Talavi filio annorum XX Talavius Tangini filius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14700019.translation: Talavius, son of Tanginus, arranged for this to be made for Tanginus, son of Talavius, aged 70, his father, and for Tanginus, son of Talavius, aged 20.
EDCS-14700019.summary: This funerary inscription records Talavius, son of Tanginus, commissioning a monument for his father Tanginus (aged 70) and another Tanginus (aged 20).

EDCS-14700021.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caio Valerio Flavo centurioni legionis VII geminae felicis Quintus Flavius Restitutus centurio heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14700021.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Flavius Restitutus, centurion, his heir, arranged for this to be made for Gaius Valerius Flávus, a centurion of the Seventh Gemina Felix legion.
EDCS-14700021.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Flavius Restitutus, as heir, commissioning a monument for Gaius Valerius Flávus, a centurion of the VII Gemina Felix legion.

EDCS-14700028.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maelo Luci Goutieni Caur\n
EDCS-14700028.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Maelus, son of Lucius Goutienus, Caur [is buried here].
EDCS-14700028.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits, likely recording the burial of Maelus, son of Lucius Goutienus.

EDCS-14700029.text_edition: \n Flavius Caesi filius annorum XXX Laurilla Lauri filio suo faciendum curavit et Fuscilla Fusci marito suo faciendum curavit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-14700029.translation: Flavius, son of Caelsius, aged 30, arranged for this to be made for Laurilla, his wife. Fuscilla, wife of Fuscus, arranged for this to be made for her husband. He lies here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-14700029.summary: This funerary inscription records Flavius (aged 30) commissioning a monument for his wife Laurilla, and Fuscilla commissioning one for her husband Fuscus, with a standard epitaph.

EDCS-14700032.text_edition: \n Gaius Cantius Modestinus Liberae et Libero animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-14700032.translation: Gaius Cantius Modestinus willingly paid his debt with a free spirit.
EDCS-14700032.summary: This inscription records Gaius Cantius Modestinus fulfilling a vow or obligation.

EDCS-15000047.translation: Caeno, son of Matsus, paid [his vow].
EDCS-15000047.summary: This inscription records Caeno, son of Matsus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-15000048.text_edition: \n Amminus Taltici Bannei Picio de voto posuit\n
EDCS-15000048.translation: Amminus, son of Talticus Banneius, set this up from a vow.
EDCS-15000048.summary: This inscription records Amminus, son of Talticus Banneius, dedicating this monument from a fulfilled vow.

EDCS-15000049.text_edition: \n Caius Sempronius Aebarus Viscunos Ni filius Cluniensis annorum X hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-15000049.translation: Gaius Sempronius Aebarus, son of Clunienus, aged 10, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-15000049.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Gaius Sempronius Aebarus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15000069.text_edition: \n Interea medium Aeneas iam classe tenebat\n
EDCS-15000069.translation: Meanwhile Aeneas was already holding the sea with his fleet.
EDCS-15000069.summary: A hexameter verse, likely from a literary work, describing Aeneas at sea with his fleet.

EDCS-15300355.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Valerius Maxsumus sic Caesaraugusta veteranus legionis VII geminae felicis nnorum LV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Valeria Maxsuma sic patri pio faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-15300355.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Valerius Maxsumus, veteran of the VII Gemina Felix legion, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Valeria Maxsuma arranged for this to be made for her devoted father.
EDCS-15300355.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Maxsumus, a veteran of a Roman legion, erected by his daughter, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300356.text_edition: \n Iovi Camilia Avita aram posuit libens animo\n
EDCS-15300356.translation: Camilia Avita placed this altar to Jupiter willingly.
EDCS-15300356.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Camilia Avita, likely a private votive offering from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300357.text_edition: \n Ariconae Alles Alionicum votum animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-15300357.translation: Ariconae Alles willingly fulfilled his vow to Alionicus.
EDCS-15300357.summary: A votive inscription by Ariconae Alles to Alionicus, possibly a local deity or hero, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15300360.text_edition: \n Iulia Avita Olisiponensis nnrum X hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Caius Iulius Quintio et Iulia filiae faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-15300360.translation: Iulia Avita of Lisbon, aged 10, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Gaius Iulius and Iulia arranged for this to be made for their daughter.
EDCS-15300360.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Iulia Avita, erected by her parents, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15700068.text_edition: \n Pintamus Turraciae annorum XLV Boeteia Urieni annorum LX hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-15700068.translation: Pintamus of Turracia, aged 45, and Boeteia Urieni, aged 60, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-15700068.summary: A joint funerary epitaph for Pintamus and Boeteia Urieni, likely a couple, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16000309.text_edition: \n Titus Pompeius Titi filius Tromentina Albinus domo Vienna IIvir tribunus militum legionis VI victricis adiutor Titi Decidi Domitiani procuratoris Caesaris Augusti hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-16000309.translation: Titus Pompeius Albinus, son of Titus, of the Tromentina tribe, from Vienna, former chief magistrate (IIvir) and military tribune of the VI Victrix legion, assistant to Titus Decidius Domitianus, procurator of Caesar Augustus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-16000309.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Pompeius Albinus, a Roman official and military man, detailing his career and origin, likely from the early Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16202015.translation: In the year 180 of the colony. Sacred to the unconquered god Mithras. Gaius Accius Hedychrus, father, placed this willingly.
EDCS-16202015.summary: A dedication to the god Mithras by Gaius Accius Hedychrus, dating to the year 180 of a Roman colony, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16202016.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Cornelio Cnaei filio Papiria Severo aedili IIviro flamini Iuliae Augustae praefecto fabrum amici X pago Augusto\n
EDCS-16202016.translation: To Gnaeus Cornelius Severus, son of Gnaeus, of the Papiria tribe, aedile, chief magistrate (IIvir), flamen of Julia Augusta, prefect of craftsmen, by his ten friends of the Augustan district.
EDCS-16202016.summary: A dedication by ten friends to Gnaeus Cornelius Severus, listing his various civic and religious offices, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16202017.text_edition: \n Invicto deo Quintio Flavi Ba<e=l>tici Coni<m=A>brigensis servo \n
EDCS-16202017.translation: To the unconquered god, from Quintius Flavius Baelticus, a servant from Conimbriga.
EDCS-16202017.summary: This is a dedication to the god Invictus, set up by Quintius Flavius Baelticus.

EDCS-16202018.text_edition: \n nno coloniae CLXXX aram Genesis Invicti Mithrae Marcus Valerius Secundus <f=I>rumentarius legionis VII geminae dono ponendam merito curavit Gaio Accio Hedychro patre \n
EDCS-16202018.translation: In the 180th year of the colony, Marcus Valerius Secundus, an instrumentarius of the VII Gemina legion, with merit, took care to erect this altar to Genesis Invictus Mithras, son of Gaius Accius Hedychrus.
EDCS-16202018.summary: This is a votive altar dedicated to Mithras in the 180th year of the colony by Marcus Valerius Secundus, a soldier.

EDCS-16202019.translation: Gaius Accius Hedychrus, father of fathers.
EDCS-16202019.summary: This records the title 'father of fathers' held by Gaius Accius Hedychrus, possibly a religious or civic honorific.

EDCS-16202020.text_edition: \n Lucius Antonius Primus cornicen Tarraconensis MANCE cornicinum \n
EDCS-16202020.translation: Lucius Antonius Primus, a cornicen from Tarraco, set up the horn.
EDCS-16202020.summary: This records the erection of a horn (possibly a monument or a military instrument) by Lucius Antonius Primus, a trumpeter.

EDCS-16400430.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Name]iae [Name]itae, aged 36 years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-16400430.summary: This is a tombstone for a woman named Iae and Itae, dated to the Roman Imperial period, with a common epitaph.

EDCS-16400431.summary: This fragmented inscription records someone named Maro returning thanks, possibly for a divine favor.

EDCS-16600003.text_edition: \n Q Mantai Bellonae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-16600003.translation: Q. Mantai willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Bellona.
EDCS-16600003.summary: This is a votive inscription by Q. Mantai to the goddess Bellona, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-16900201.translation: Procula, aged 11 years. Fuscus, his fellow freedman.
EDCS-16900201.summary: This epitaph records the death of Procula at age 11 and commemorates her relation to Fuscus.

EDCS-16900205.text_edition: \n Lucius Maecius Scepticus nnorum LXXX Laberia Daphne uxor nnorum LXcum qua vixit annis LII Lucius Laberius Antigonus utrorumque filius nnorum LXX faciendum curavit hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis Cornelia Valentina nnorum XXXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-16900205.translation: Lucius Maecius Scepticus, aged 80 years. Laberia Daphne, his wife, aged 60 years, with whom he lived for 52 years. Lucius Laberius Antigonus, son of both, aged 70 years, saw to the construction. Here they lie; may the earth lie lightly upon you. Cornelia Valentina, aged 32 years, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-16900205.summary: This is a large family tomb inscription for Lucius Maecius Scepticus, his wife Laberia Daphne, their son Lucius Laberius Antigonus, and Cornelia Valentina, likely another family member.

EDCS-16900206.text_edition: \n Publius Sertorius Niger medicus sibi et Publio Sertorio patri suo et Caeciliae Urbanae uxori suae Sertoriae Tertullae sorori suae et Marcus Didius Postumus sobrinus et heres Publi Sertori Nigri de suo sibi statuam posuit\n
EDCS-16900206.translation: Publius Sertorius Niger, the doctor, for himself and for his father Publius Sertorius, and for his wife Caecilia Urbana, and for his sister Sertoria Tertulla, and Marcus Didius Postumus, nephew and heir of Publius Sertorius Niger, erected a statue for them at his own expense.
EDCS-16900206.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription detailing the erection of a statue by Publius Sertorius Niger, a doctor, for himself and his family members.

EDCS-16900207.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Sertoria Elpis, aged 50. Anicius Marcellianus had [it] made.
EDCS-16900207.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sertoria Elpis, aged 50, commissioned by Anicius Marcellianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100150.translation: Compedio, aged 25. Sacred to the Divine Shades.
EDCS-18100150.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Compedio, aged 25, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18100384.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified [Julius Caesar], grandson of Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power V, consul III, rebuilt [this road] for 18 miles.
EDCS-18100384.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a road for 18 miles by Emperor Hadrian, mentioning his titles and consulships, dating to his reign (117-138 CE).

EDCS-19100716.translation: [...] Adfestus, aged 10. Cilea, his mother, had [this monument] made. He is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-19100716.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Adfestus, aged 10, set up by his mother Cilea, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19100726.translation: Severa, daughter of Muntanus, aged 65, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. // Catuenus, son [of...?], aged 30, is laid here. [May] the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-19100726.summary: This inscription contains two funerary epitaphs: one for Severa, aged 65, and another for Catuenus, aged 30, both from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19100727.translation: Macana, daughter of Tureius, aged 60. Camalus, son of Maduus, aged 20, are laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you both. Allo, son of Maduus, had [this monument] made for [his] mother.
EDCS-19100727.summary: This inscription records two burials, Macana and Camalus, with a dedication by Allo for his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19100729.translation: Aper, son of Boutus, is laid here. Sunua, daughter of Melamanus, and Boutia, daughter of Aper, [3]atv[...]
EDCS-19100729.summary: This inscription lists the burials of Aper and Sunua, and possibly Boutia, with paternal and maternal lineage noted, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19100731.translation: Boutia, daughter of Aibarus, aged 22, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Tongi, son of Tancinus, had [this monument] made.
EDCS-19100731.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Boutia, aged 22, commissioned by Tongi, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19100733.translation: Tureus, son of Cains, aged [3?] years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you. His brother [3?]pio took care to have it made.
EDCS-19100733.summary: A funerary inscription for Tureus, son of Cains, with a dedication from his brother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19200002.text_edition: \n Deibueran DN ANGIITA AN INI fili-- VISVA faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-19200002.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Angiita, daughter of Deibueran, Visva took care to have it made.
EDCS-19200002.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Angiita, daughter of Deibueran, with the maker identified as Visva, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19200003.text_edition: \n Aponia Narcissa hic sita est sit tibi terra levis B Narcisso OS\n
EDCS-19200003.translation: Aponia Narcissa is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you. To Narcissus, from OS.
EDCS-19200003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aponia Narcissa, with a dedication to Narcissus.

EDCS-20400046.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum mater posuit filio Cattusio memoriam vixsit sic annis duodecim\n
EDCS-20400046.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. A mother placed this in memory of her son Cattusius, who lived for twelve years.
EDCS-20400046.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating Cattusius, set up by his mother.

EDCS-20400053.translation: Phoebus, freedman of the Vibii Popillii, Iuventus and Primula.
EDCS-20400053.summary: This inscription likely records a freedman named Phoebus and his patrons from the Vibii Popillii family.

EDCS-20400055.text_edition: \n Iulia Maura nnorum LXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Turrania Beronice matri de suo fecit\n
EDCS-20400055.translation: Iulia Maura, aged 65, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Turrania Beronice made this for her mother at her own expense.
EDCS-20400055.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Maura, set up by her daughter Turrania Beronice.

EDCS-20400057.translation: [...]Vitu[?]a, for Publius Attennius Ama[?], Augustalis. Gaius Attennius, son of Publius [...].
EDCS-20400057.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning Publius Attennius Ama[?], an Augustalis, and Gaius Attennius.

EDCS-20400058.translation: Gaius Julius Fronto placed this for Vitula, who deserved well, at his own expense.
EDCS-20400058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Gaius Julius Fronto for the deserving Vitula.

EDCS-20400165.text_edition: \n Lucius Afinius Modestus veteranus legionis VII geminae Felicis \n
EDCS-20400165.translation: Lucius Afinius Modestus, veteran of the VII Gemina Felix legion.
EDCS-20400165.summary: This records Lucius Afinius Modestus, a veteran of a specific Roman legion.

EDCS-20400175.translation: To the Lord God Mercury, Gaius Potius Maximus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-20400175.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mercury, dedicated by Gaius Potius Maximus.

EDCS-20400196.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Arco, son of Camalus, aged 40. Victorina, daughter of Camalus, arranged for her brother to be buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20400196.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arco, son of Camalus, with arrangements made by his sister Victorina.

EDCS-20400321.text_edition: \n Caesariciaeco Duatius Bovi filius Fraternu<s=M> votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-20400321.translation: Caesariceaco, Duatius Bovi's son Fraternus, willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-20400321.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Duatius Bovi's son, Fraternus, possibly related to the imperial family or a place name.

EDCS-20400333.translation: To Boutia, daughter of Cloutus, aged 40. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20400333.summary: A funerary epitaph for a woman named Boutia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400416.translation: Flavus, son of Pressus, of the Ataecini tribe, willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-20400416.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Flavus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400417.translation: To Amia, freedwoman of Hispañus, daughter of Uriloucus. She willingly fulfilled her vow as deserved.
EDCS-20400417.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Amia, a freedwoman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400510.translation: The garden of Reburrus, son of Publius.
EDCS-20400510.summary: A boundary marker for a garden belonging to Reburrus, son of Publius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401565.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iolumii Fabia Ser hic situs Iolumiu pater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-20401565.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iolumius Pater, son of Iolumius, saw to the making of this (tomb) for his father.
EDCS-20401565.summary: A funerary monument erected by Iolumius Pater for his father, Iolumius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401568.text_edition: \n Tancinus Cili filius annorum XXII hic situs est Cilius pater Avita mater faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-20401568.translation: Tancinus, son of Cilius, aged 22, lies here. Cilius, his father, and Avita, his mother, saw to the making of this (tomb).
EDCS-20401568.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tancinus, son of Cilius, with his parents named, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401570.text_edition: \n Boutia Camali filia annorum XL hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401570.translation: Boutia, daughter of Camalus, aged 40, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20401570.summary: A funerary epitaph for Boutia, daughter of Camalus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401572.text_edition: \n Caio Voconio Cai filio Papiria patri Caeciliae Anui matri Voconiae Cai filiae Mariae sorori Caius Voconius Cai filius Proculus fecit\n
EDCS-20401572.translation: To Gaius Vocontius Proculus, son of Gaius, son of Gaius, his father, and Caecilia Anui, his mother, and Voconia Maria, daughter of Gaius, his sister. Gaius Vocontius Proculus made this.
EDCS-20401572.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Vocontius Proculus for his deceased father, mother, and sister, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401573.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai libertus Felix Quinta Caecilia |(mulieris) liberta Mauriola sit tibi terra levis Caius Iulius Modestus nnorum XXVII\n
EDCS-20401573.translation: To Gaius Julius Felix, freedman of Gaius Julius, and Quinta Caecilia Mauriola, freedwoman of a woman (or Gaius Julius). May the earth lie lightly upon you. Gaius Julius Modestus, aged 27.
EDCS-20401573.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Felix and Quinta Caecilia Mauriola, with Gaius Julius Modestus mentioned, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401578.text_edition: \n Camira Chilonis filia annorum LXX mater Amoena Antubeli filia annorum L hic sitae sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-20401578.translation: Camira, daughter of Chilo, aged 70, and Amoena, daughter of Antubelius, aged 50, lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you both.
EDCS-20401578.summary: A funerary epitaph for two women, Camira and Amoena, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401580.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Probi Neirobini annorum XIX hic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401580.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Probus, son of Neirobius, aged nineteen years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20401580.summary: A funerary epitaph for Probus, son of Neirobius, who died at nineteen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401581.translation: Ania, daughter of Rufus, aged twelve years.
EDCS-20401581.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ania, daughter of Rufus, who died at twelve, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401582.text_edition: \n Medugenus Morti annorum LXI sit tibi terra levis Reburrus Ambati nnorum XI sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401582.translation: Medugenus, son of Mortius, aged sixty-one years. May the earth lie lightly on you. Reburrus, son of Ambatus, aged eleven years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20401582.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burials of two individuals, Medugenus and Reburrus, with ages provided, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401583.text_edition: \n Iuliae Adiae Fusci filiae annorum LX Bouta soror faciendum curavit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401583.translation: To Julia Adia, daughter of Fuscus, aged sixty years. Her sister Bouta took care to have it made. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20401583.summary: A funerary monument for Julia Adia, daughter of Fuscus, funded by her sister Bouta, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401584.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Placidi Ianni annorum LX\n
EDCS-20401584.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Placidus, son of Iannus, aged sixty years.
EDCS-20401584.summary: A funerary epitaph for Placidus, son of Iannus, who died at sixty, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401585.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Annius A [---]
EDCS-20401585.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Annius A, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401587.summary: A brief funerary record for Compedia, aged sixty-five, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401588.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Alaino annorum LXVI sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401588.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Alainus, aged sixty-six years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-20401588.summary: A funerary inscription for Alainus, aged sixty-six, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20401613.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Serenus Celti filius annorum LX hic tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401613.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Serenus, son of Celtus, aged sixty, may the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-20401613.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Serenus, son of Celtus, who lived to be sixty years old.

EDCS-20401614.text_edition: \n Ama nnorum LX hic tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401614.translation: Amanus, aged sixty, may the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-20401614.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Amanus, who lived to be sixty years old.

EDCS-20401615.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aulius R Quinti annorum XXI Dis Manibus\n
EDCS-20401615.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Aulus R, son of Quintus, aged 21. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-20401615.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus R, aged 21, dedicated to the departed spirits.

EDCS-21700238.translation: Publius Talius, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, a soldier in the 10th legion, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700238.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Talius, a soldier in the 10th legion, indicating his burial place and wishing him a light earth.

EDCS-21700247.text_edition: \n Elea Tancini filia hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-21700247.translation: Elea, daughter of Tancinus, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-21700247.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Elea, daughter of Tancinus, indicating her burial place and wishing her a light earth.

EDCS-21700249.text_edition: \n Epoamonius Modestus annorum XXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis EI FA QV\n
EDCS-21700249.translation: Epoamonius Modestus, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. EI FA QV
EDCS-21700249.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Epoamonius Modestus, aged 35, indicating his burial place and wishing him a light earth.

EDCS-21700250.translation: Dedicated to the departed spirits. Roscia.
EDCS-21700250.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits for Roscia.

EDCS-21700269.translation: Maelo, son of Caeno, aged 80. Caeno, son of Maelo, aged 50. Cabureina, daughter of Caeno.
EDCS-21700269.summary: This inscription records the burial of Maelo (son of Caeno, 80 years old) and Caeno (son of Maelo, 50 years old), and mentions Cabureina (daughter of Caeno).

EDCS-21700660.text_edition: \n ------] Praesente et Extricato II consulibus Vegetus Talabari filius aedilis sacravit\n
EDCS-21700660.translation: With Praesens and Extricatus as consuls, Vegetus, son of Talabarus, as aedile, dedicated this.
EDCS-21700660.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a public work by Vegetus, son of Talabarus, during the consulship of Praesens and Extricatus, when he served as aedile.

EDCS-21900102.translation: [...]nas, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Mother and father took care to have this made at their own expense.
EDCS-21900102.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a person identified by the incomplete name '[...]nas', aged 18, with parents commissioning the monument.

EDCS-21900122.text_edition: \n Calaetus Coropoti filius hic situs est\n
EDCS-21900122.translation: Calaetus, son of Coropotus, is buried here.
EDCS-21900122.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Calaetus, son of Coropotus, indicating his burial place.

EDCS-21900126.translation: Maternus, son of Tancinus, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-21900126.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Maternus, son of Tancinus, aged 25, indicating his burial place.

EDCS-22200064.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae Hector Corneliorum ex visu\n
EDCS-22200064.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Hector received this vision.
EDCS-22200064.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the god Mithras, possibly by Hector who experienced a vision.

EDCS-22200147.text_edition: \n Mantae hic sit-- est sit tibi terra levis Manta pater Amoena mater faciendum curaverunt I C\n
EDCS-22200147.translation: Manta, may the earth lie lightly upon you here. Manta, father, and Amoena, mother, took care to have this made. I.C.
EDCS-22200147.summary: A tombstone for Manta, with his parents Manta and Amoena commissioning the monument.

EDCS-22200148.translation: To Tancius, son of Celtius, son of Ente/ranus, aged sixty-five, here.
EDCS-22200148.summary: A tombstone for Tancius, son of Celtius, who died at the age of sixty-five.

EDCS-22200149.translation: Saturninus, son of Proculus, aged seventeen, is laid here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22200149.summary: A tombstone for Saturninus, son of Proculus, who died at the age of seventeen.

EDCS-22200361.translation: Julia Modesta, daughter of Lucius, aged eighteen. Livia Nymphe, aged forty. Lucius Julius Thymelicus had them, his daughters and wife, laid here for himself.
EDCS-22200361.summary: A tombstone commissioned by Lucius Julius Thymelicus for himself, his wife Livia Nymphe, and his daughter Julia Modesta.

EDCS-22200364.translation: The Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified one, consul for the 11th time, imperator for the 8th time.
EDCS-22200364.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely from the time of Emperor Augustus, recording his titles and consulships.

EDCS-22200373.text_edition: \n Rufinus Calaeti annorum LX (mensium) V (dierum) X amici faciendum curaverunt Dis Manibus sacrum sit tibi terra levis hic cubat\n
EDCS-22200373.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Rufinus, son of Caletus, aged sixty, five months, and ten days. Friends took care to have this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you. He rests here.
EDCS-22200373.summary: A tombstone for Rufinus, son of Caletus, who died at the age of sixty years, five months, and ten days, commissioned by his friends.

EDCS-22200377.text_edition: \n Rufus Rufi annorum X hic situs est\n
EDCS-22200377.translation: Rufus, son of Rufus, aged ten, is laid here.
EDCS-22200377.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Rufus, son of Rufus, who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-22200382.text_edition: \n Quiatia Cumi Rotamus Tritei Cat votum merito\n
EDCS-22200382.translation: Quiatia Cumi, fulfilling a vow to Rotamus Tritei, has done this.
EDCS-22200382.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a dedication made by Quiatia Cumi, likely for a deity named Rotamus Tritei.

EDCS-22400215.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Decimus Julius Pecio is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Ebrilia Secunda made this for her most devoted husband, and Decimus Julius Euphrosdoction for his excellent father.
EDCS-22400215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decimus Julius Pecio, erected by his wife Ebrilia Secunda, and also commemorating Decimus Julius Euphrosdoction, likely his son.

EDCS-22400216.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Valerius Salutaris, aged 56. Valeria Bassina made this for her most loving husband.
EDCS-22400216.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Valerius Salutaris, dedicated by his wife Valeria Bassina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400218.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Muciae Rodope, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Oc[3]ius Pro[3]n() made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-22400218.summary: A funerary epitaph for Muciae Rodope, indicating she is buried here and the monument was erected by her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400451.translation: Dovitena, daughter of Nepos, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22400451.summary: A funerary epitaph for Dovitena, daughter of Nepos, indicating she is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400452.translation: Paterno, son of Camalus, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22400452.summary: A funerary epitaph for Paterno, son of Camalus, indicating he is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400453.translation: Turovius Ambini, aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-22400453.summary: A funerary epitaph for Turovius Ambini, indicating he is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22400454.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aelius Mao[3], aged [---].
EDCS-22400454.summary: A fragment of a funerary epitaph for Aelius Mao[3], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22600031.text_edition: \n Imperatore Tito VIII consule fontem aedificavit Severus Vituli filius\n
EDCS-22600031.translation: In the eighth consulship of Emperor Titus, Severus, son of Vitulus, built the fountain.
EDCS-22600031.summary: An inscription recording the construction of a fountain by Severus, son of Vitulus, during the consulship of Titus (AD 80), indicating a public works project.

EDCS-22800028.text_edition: \n Quintus Blaesienus Quinti filius Sergia Potitus annorum XXXI hic situs est\n
EDCS-22800028.translation: Quintus Blaesienus, son of Quintus, of the Sergia tribe, Potitus, aged 31, is buried here.
EDCS-22800028.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Blaesienus Potitus, indicating he is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800030.translation: Asellia Hygia, aged 39. Marcus Publicius Felix. They rest here, loving each other. May the earth lie lightly upon you. From the will, for the most dutiful daughter Asellia Claudia.
EDCS-22800030.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Publicius Felix and Asellia Hygia, likely erected by their daughter from their will, indicating burial and affection, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800031.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Octavius Sperchius Phryx secutor nnorum XXIIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Acilia Aurora \n
EDCS-22800031.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Octavius Sperchius, a Phrygian gladiator (secutor), aged 24, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Acilia Aurora.
EDCS-22800031.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Octavius Sperchius, a gladiator, indicating he is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800115.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Valeria T[---], 55 years of age. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Arru[ntius] Paederos [erected this] for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-22800115.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria T[---] set up by her husband, Arru[ntius] Paederos, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800117.text_edition: \n Sunua Celti filia annorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis pater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-22800117.translation: Sunua, daughter of Celtus, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her father arranged for her burial.
EDCS-22800117.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sunua, daughter of Celtus, erected by her father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800361.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Dacciliae Tanginiae filiae memoria! de se annorum XXV mater possuit sic\n
EDCS-22800361.translation: Sacred to the shades of the underworld. Her mother set up this memorial for Daccilia Tanginia, daughter of [---], aged 25.
EDCS-22800361.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Daccilia Tanginia, set up by her mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22900027.text_edition: \n Caio Cantio Modesto Caius Cantius Modestinus patri\n
EDCS-22900027.translation: To Gaius Cantinus Modestus. Gaius Cantinus Modestinus [erected this] for his father.
EDCS-22900027.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Cantinus Modestus, erected by his son, Gaius Cantinus Modestinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23001965.translation: Aemilia, daughter of Mani, of the Cetti family, lies here.
EDCS-23001965.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aemilia, daughter of Mani, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-23001979.translation: Gaius Salvius, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, lies here. Frontage 12 feet, depth 10 feet.
