EDCS-63100007.translation: Maxumus, son of Muntanus, aged 66, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His sons saw to the making of this monument.
EDCS-63100007.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maxumus, erected by his sons, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-63100008.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, incomplete, for Sextus Julius Proculus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-63100009.translation: ...Saturninus of Emerita, aged 17. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-63100009.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Saturninus of Emerita, aged 17, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-63100010.translation: Lucius Sarius Celer, aged 40, son (?), saw to the making of this monument. He lies here.
EDCS-63100010.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Sarius Celer, erected by himself or a relative, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-63100011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, incomplete, for Faventius Lucullus, son of Lucius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-63100019.translation: Sacred to the departed gods. Quintus Atinius Cai (?), aged 80, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Britta Floria saw to the making of this monument out of devotion.
EDCS-63100019.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Atinius Cai, erected by Britta Floria out of devotion, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-63100023.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cilea Visali, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-63600347.translation: Marcus Julius Piso, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 3, lies here.
EDCS-63600347.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the young Marcus Julius Piso, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-63600348.translation: To the departed gods. Licinia Maela, daughter of Marcus, lies here. She made this at her own expense.
EDCS-63600348.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinia Maela, who funded her own burial, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-63600351.translation: Voluscia Tusca, daughter of Gaius, lies here. [
EDCS-63600351.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, incomplete, for Voluscia Tusca, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-64700343.translation: To the shades of the departed. Partica lived 31 years, 6 months, and 28 days. Merita made [this] for her husband. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-64700343.summary: A funerary epitaph for Partica, who lived 31 years, 6 months, and 28 days, dedicated by Merita.

EDCS-64900623.translation: Maecia Pelli, daughter, 70 years old, here may she rest, may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-64900623.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maecia Pelli, aged 70.

EDCS-64900624.translation: Here may he rest, may the earth rest lightly upon [you]. Saini.
EDCS-64900624.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph possibly for someone named Saini.

EDCS-64900626.translation: [Here lies] Valerius, [of age] ... Valeri... most pious patron, here he lies, may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-64900626.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Valerius, described as a most pious patron.

EDCS-64900634.translation: To the shades of the departed. Lucius? Po... Natalis(?), 50 years.
EDCS-64900634.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Lucius? Po... Natalis(?), aged 50.

EDCS-64900635.translation: Briseis, [aged] ... here she lies, may the earth rest lightly upon you. Symphorus had this made for his wife.
EDCS-64900635.summary: A funerary epitaph for Briseis, for whom her husband Symphorus commissioned the monument.

EDCS-64900638.translation: Iulia Amoena, daughter of Quintus, aged 20 years, here she lies, may the earth rest lightly upon you. Terentia Aunia, daughter of Publius, her mother, had this made.
EDCS-64900638.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Amoena, aged 20, commissioned by her mother Terentia Aunia.

EDCS-64900639.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, likely part of a dedication or epitaph, mentioning a daughter of Docqur.

EDCS-64900642.translation: To the shades of the departed. Cila, 60 years old, here may she rest, may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-64900642.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cila, aged 60.

EDCS-64900647.translation: For Caenon, son of Doqur, 25 years old... had [this] made for his mother... here he lies.
EDCS-64900647.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caenon, son of Doqur, aged 25, commissioned by his mother.

EDCS-64900654.translation: Sever[---]nis to the gods of Calobedai, a native deity (?), willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-64900654.summary: This is a votive inscription from an unknown dedicator to a local deity, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64900656.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Marcus Egna(tius?) Max(imus?), aged 15, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. His grandmother arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-64900656.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Marcus Egna(tius?) Max(imus?), erected by his grandmother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65700890.translation: [---]acia, daughter of [---], aged [---], lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Cilea arranged for this to be made for her mother [---].
EDCS-65700890.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named [---]acia, with a second deceased woman, Cilea, mentioned as the commissioner, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65700894.translation: For Duatia, daughter of L() Goutus, aged 40, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Albinus, her husband, willingly arranged for this to be made from his own funds [dear?] to all her family. L() Goutina, daughter of Albinus, aged 20, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Albinus, her devoted father, arranged for this to be made for his daughter [or mother?].
EDCS-65700894.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription recording the burial of two women, Duatia and L() Goutina, with Albinus acting as the commissioner for both, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000021.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Agatemerus, aged 14, lies here; may the earth be light upon you. Botola made this.
EDCS-66000021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Agatemerus, erected by Botola, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000024.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [---] Carpetienus Arbaiza, son, and Donata, daughter, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-66000024.summary: This is a funerary inscription where siblings, Carpetienus Arbaiza and Donata, commissioned a monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000025.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [---]SE[---], aged 35, Callae[---], for his merits [or merits of her family?], as a relative, arranged for this to be made; may the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-66000025.summary: This funerary inscription records a person named [---]SE[---] (or possibly Callae[---]), aged 35, with the monument commissioned by relatives due to merits, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000027.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cornelia Euche lived [---] 36 years [---].
EDCS-66000027.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for Cornelia Euche, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000028.translation: For Flavia [---]. Secundus, her husband, made this. She lies here [---].
EDCS-66000028.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavia, erected by her husband Secundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000030.summary: This is a severely fragmented inscription with an unknown purpose, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000032.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lu...
EDCS-66000032.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Spirits of the Departed, likely from a tomb, but it is too incomplete to determine further details.

EDCS-66000033.translation: Medugena, daughter of Medugena, may the earth lie lightly upon you here.
EDCS-66000033.summary: This funerary inscription records Medugena, daughter of Medugena, with a common epitaph wishing her light earth, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000034.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publicia Atta had this made for her son Publius Fabatus and for herself.
EDCS-66000034.summary: This funerary inscription records a mother, Publicia Atta, commissioning a monument for her son Publius Fabatus and herself, dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed.

EDCS-66000035.translation: [...]rano. Reburrus son of Arron had this made.
EDCS-66000035.summary: This inscription records Reburrus son of Arron commissioning a monument, likely funerary, but the beginning is lost.

EDCS-66000036.translation: [...] son Vitulus, aged 50. Paternius Aper had this made for his son.
EDCS-66000036.summary: This inscription records Paternius Aper commissioning a monument for his son Vitulus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-66000037.translation: [...] son of [X]ice, aged 30, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-66000037.summary: This funerary inscription records an individual, son of [X]ice, aged 30, being buried here, with a wish for light earth, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66000116.summary: This incomplete inscription is a funerary dedication to Avinia, daughter or wife of Marcus, but lacks further details.

EDCS-66000117.translation: Turenda, daughter of Valutus Vivatus, aged 7. Tongius...
EDCS-66000117.summary: This funerary inscription records the young Turenda, daughter of Valutus Vivatus, aged 7, with an incomplete mention of Tongius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66100003.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. A[X]us son of Mustarus had this made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-66100003.summary: This funerary inscription, dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed, records A[X]us son of Mustarus commissioning a monument and includes a wish for light earth.

EDCS-66300067.translation: Lucius Coutilius Bandi, freedman of Bulcai, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-66300067.summary: This inscription records Lucius Coutilius Bandi, a freedman, willingly fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-66300083.translation: Here lies Iunius, of Cluni origin, aged 13.
EDCS-66300083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Iunius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-66300084.translation: Coria, daughter of Alluquius, aged 42, is buried here; Mailo arranged for the construction for his mother Quertia (?).
EDCS-66300084.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Coria, commissioned by her son Mailo, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-67400271.translation: To the shades below. Agape Argentariorum (?), aged 30. Sorica made this for her most devoted sister; she is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-67400271.summary: This funerary inscription honors Agape, who died at age 30, and was dedicated by her sister Sorica, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-67400272.translation: To the shades below, sacred. Claudia Patricia arranged for the construction for her patron Caudius Silvanus at her own expense.
EDCS-67400272.summary: This funerary inscription records Claudia Patricia's arrangement for a monument for her patron, Caudius Silvanus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-67400273.translation: To the shades below, sacred. Cornelius Oristis [---] for her husband [---]
EDCS-67400273.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, likely recording the death of a husband and the person who set up the monument, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-67400274.translation: Titus Marius Secundus, aged 40, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Leuche, a slave of Atilia Tertulla, aged 24, and Mistiche, a homeborn slave, aged 1 year and 6 months, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you both.
EDCS-67400274.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Titus Marius Secundus and two enslaved women, Leuche and Mistiche, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-67400275.translation: [---], aged 17, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-67400275.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a young person aged 17, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-67400276.translation: [---] his wife [---] Publius Aelius, son of Publius, B[---] Aelius A[---] for his father [---]
EDCS-67400276.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a funerary monument, mentioning a wife and a father-son relationship, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-67400278.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, possibly the beginning of a name or dedication, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-67400281.translation: [---] is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Surina and Patern[a] [---]
EDCS-67400281.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription mentioning Surina and Patern[a], dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-67400282.translation: Imperator Caesar Augustus, son of the deified one, consul for the 11th time, imperator for the 10th time.
EDCS-67400282.summary: This is an abbreviated titulature of Emperor Augustus, likely part of a larger inscription or monument, dating to his reign (27 BC - 14 AD).

EDCS-67500080.summary: This inscription likely records a deification or honors bestowed upon the deified Emperor Augustus by Emperor Vespasian, who also deified his own predecessors.

EDCS-67900817.translation: Quinta Caecilius, son of Quinta, Segossus, and Sextus Caecilius, son of Sextus Teirus, as aediles, saw to its construction.
EDCS-67900817.summary: This inscription records the construction of a wall and altar by two aediles, Sextus Caecilius Teirus and Quintus Caecilius Segossus, probably during the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-68300182.translation: Quintus Nonius Pri[...], soldier of the 7th Gemina Felix legion, willingly fulfilled his vow to the August Lares.
EDCS-68300182.summary: This inscription records a soldier of the Legio VII Gemina Felix, Quintus Nonius Pri[...], fulfilling a vow to the Lares Augusti, dating to after the legion's formation in the 1st century AD.

EDCS-68300183.translation: Lucius Claudius Donatus placed this in fulfillment of a vow to the Holy Goddess Proserpina.
EDCS-68300183.summary: This inscription records Lucius Claudius Donatus dedicating a statue or monument to the goddess Proserpina in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-68600111.translation: Publilia Haline, freedwoman of Caius, will not be followed by an heir to this monument or tomb.
EDCS-68600111.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publilia Haline, a freedwoman, stating that the tomb is not to be inherited, typical for private burials.

EDCS-68600112.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. A husband made this for his most devoted and most frugal wife, [who lived] 36 years.
EDCS-68600112.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a wife, made by her husband, recording her virtues and age at death, dating from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-68700049.translation: Mauria, daughter of Valentinus, aged 25 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-68700049.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Mauria, daughter of Valentinus, recording her name, parentage, age, and a common funerary wish, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-69100244.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus [...].
EDCS-69100244.summary: This is the beginning of an inscription honoring Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantinus (Constantine the Great), dating to his reign (306-337 AD).

EDCS-69100245.translation: Cassius Iustus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, formerly a decurion, aedile, and flamen, [died aged] 75 years. He lies here.
EDCS-69100245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cassius Iustus, recording his career in local magistracies (decurion, aedile, flamen) and his age at death, dating likely to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-69100247.translation: Jano [---] his wife [---] with her own money [---]
EDCS-69100247.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a dedication or memorial, possibly funded by a wife.

EDCS-69200097.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Parda, aged 45. Lupatus caused this to be made for his most devoted mother. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-69200097.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Parda at age 45 and was erected by her son, Lupatus.

EDCS-69200099.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Victor Vitalis for his most devoted wife Camira, who lived 29 years.
EDCS-69200099.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Camira at age 29 and was dedicated by her husband, Victor Vitalis.

EDCS-69200315.translation: Tritius, aged 27. Buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-69200315.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Tritius, aged 27.

EDCS-69200316.translation: Love, to Dobitero, son of Cloutus, aged 30.
EDCS-69200316.summary: This inscription is an epitaph recording the death of Dobitero, son of Cloutus, at the age of 30.

EDCS-69200317.summary: This is a partial funerary epitaph recording a person's age as 35, with missing identifying information.

EDCS-69200318.translation: Arrenus [---] aged [---]. Buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-69200318.summary: This fragmentary epitaph records that Arrenus was buried here, with his age missing.

EDCS-69400009.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Fundanus [---] for 11 months and 11 days. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-69400009.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Fundanus, which was 11 months and 11 days.

EDCS-69400011.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Kalvina, aged 70. Manius, her son, made this for his most devoted mother.
EDCS-69400011.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Kalvina at age 70 and was erected by her son, Manius.

EDCS-69400012.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Daphnus, aged 50. Valeria Hebene caused this to be made for her most devoted husband. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-69400012.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Daphnus at age 50 and was erected by his wife, Valeria Hebene.

EDCS-69400014.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavia Patricia, freedwoman of Flavius Primigenius, aged 12, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-69400014.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Flavia Patricia, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70600112.translation: To Diana. Valeria Terpne willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-70600112.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Diana by Valeria Terpne, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70800033.translation: Lucius [erected this] for Erois, his most pious wife, daughter of Apaena, aged 20. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-70800033.summary: A funerary epitaph for Erois, a woman aged 20, dedicated by her husband Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-70900414.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name Niger and an age, likely a funerary text from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71100008.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Turuca Amana, aged 72. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-71100008.summary: A funerary epitaph for Turuca Amana, who died at the age of 72, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71100009.translation: [This is here?] for Iulia Surillionis, daughter of Marcellus, aged 50. May the earth lie lightly upon you. [Her son?] arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-71100009.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Marcell a, daughter of Surillionis, aged 50, with a mention of a son arranging the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71100010.translation: Coria Ebur[i], aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. C[...?], a friend, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-71100010.summary: A funerary epitaph for Coria Eburi, aged 60, with a friend arranging the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71200463.translation: To Caecilius Rusticus, son of Velabius, of the Sergius tribe, aedile and duumvir. Vernio, his freedman, [erected this].
EDCS-71200463.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Caecilius Rusticus, who held the offices of aedile and duumvir, erected by his freedman Vernio, likely dating to the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-71400404.translation: [...] freedwoman of Marcus, [...] freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-71400404.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning two freedwomen, one of Marcus and one of Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71400407.translation: To the spirits. Aeicatus, son of Boelius. Rufus and A[...] dedicated [this statue?].
EDCS-71400407.summary: A dedication to the spirits by Aeicatus, son of Boelius, with Rufus and A[...] as dedicants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71400409.translation: Pella, daughter of [---], aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. [---] and [---] took care to have this made.
EDCS-71400409.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Pella, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71500119.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly a dedication or funerary epitaph, honoring Antonio, son of [---].

EDCS-71500129.translation: Lucius Caenobius Severianus, son of Quintus, and his daughter Iulia Rufa took care to have this made. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-71500129.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Caenobius Severianus, with his daughter Iulia Rufa arranging the burial, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72000002.translation: His sons took care to have this made for Celtius, son of Caturonis.
EDCS-72000002.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where sons honor their father Celtius, son of Caturonis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72000003.translation: Cilus, son of Pisirus, is buried here. [---]
EDCS-72000003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cilus, son of Pisirus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72000004.translation: L[---], aged 35, C() Augusta placed this.
EDCS-72000004.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for L[---], aged 35, erected by C() Augusta, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72300319.translation: To the Holy Goddesses/Lady. Lucius Annius Severus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-72300319.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Annius Severus to a female deity or deities, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72300320.translation: Lucius Tussanius Rufus, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, aged 16, is buried here.
EDCS-72300320.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Tussanius Rufus, a young man of 16, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72600197.translation: [---]io, possibly Limia, is buried here. Al() possibly Limiae, for his brother, took care to have this made, under the care of Cil().
EDCS-72600197.summary: This is a funerary epitaph mentioning burial and construction arrangements for individuals possibly named Limia and Al(), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72600198.translation: [---]s, son of Silinus, from Isienses(?), dedicated a bull to the god Jupiter Talabrigus, [---] successfully.
EDCS-72600198.summary: This is a dedication of a bull to Jupiter Talabrigus by a man named Silinus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72600200.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Titus Flavius Graptus built the monument.

EDCS-72600212.translation: To the departed spirits. Favius Musanus, aged thirty.
EDCS-72600212.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Favius Musanus, who died at the age of thirty.

EDCS-72700012.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a son of Flacinus, identified as Salus.

EDCS-72700014.translation: A monument for the altar. Cloutaius, son of Munitonis, Lusca, daughter of Agapitus, and Statilius Capito, son of Boius, set this up and wrote the inscription with willing hearts.
EDCS-72700014.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument and altar by Cloutaius, Lusca, and Statilius Capito, likely family members, for the deceased.

EDCS-72700056.translation: To the departed spirits. [Father] Domitius [made this] for his daughter Hygi[---], aged three.
EDCS-72700056.summary: This is a funerary inscription where a father, likely Domitius, made this monument for his daughter who died at age three.

EDCS-72700057.translation: Maxsuma, daughter of Tongatus, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Aelius Ta[...]
EDCS-72700057.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Maxsuma, daughter of Tongatus, with a common epitaph and the beginning of a name, possibly the dedicator.

EDCS-72800092.translation: [Primula, daughter of Varius?], arranged for this to be made for her most devoted son, Lauro, son of Rufus.
EDCS-72800092.summary: This funerary inscription records that Primula arranged for the construction of a monument for her devoted son, Lauro.

EDCS-72800093.translation: [He lies] here, aged 105. Maxsuma, daughter of [...], arranged for this to be made. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-72800093.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a person aged 105, with Maxsuma arranging for the monument, and includes a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-72800131.translation: Teusca, daughter of Petreius, freely fulfilled her vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-72800131.summary: This inscription records Teusca, daughter of Petreius, fulfilling a votive offering to Jupiter.

EDCS-72900111.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that begins by identifying Quintus Norbanus, son of Publius.

EDCS-73300080.translation: Triteus, son of Mantai, aged 65, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-73300080.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Triteus, who died at the age of 65, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73300082.translation: Silo's son, aged 52, and Camira, daughter of Silo, aged 2, lie here. May the earth lie lightly on you both.
EDCS-73300082.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two individuals, likely a father and daughter, indicating their ages and resting place, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73300083.translation: Coria, daughter of Alluquus(?), aged 42, lies here. Mailo had it made for his most beloved mother.
EDCS-73300083.summary: This epitaph records the death of Coria at age 42 and was commissioned by her son Mailo, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100211.translation: [---]panus Maximus, aged 72, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-74100211.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a man named Maximus, who died at the age of 72, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100212.translation: Balbus, son of Mani, Colarnus, aged 70. Tritus, son, to his father.
EDCS-74100212.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with a partial dedication from Tritus to his father Balbus, who died at the age of 70, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74100219.translation: Neritus, slave of Maria Prisca, died here aged 20. Likewise Nereis, his sister, lies here aged 25. You who strive, weary traveler, [---] PROCOBV[---]
EDCS-74100219.summary: This incomplete epitaph records the deaths of a slave named Neritus and his sister Nereis, and includes an address to the traveler, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200044.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Rufinus, son of Rufus, aged 15, and Rufus, son of Rufinus, aged 30, [---]
EDCS-74200044.summary: This incomplete funerary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes and records two individuals, Rufinus and Rufus, with their ages, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200164.translation: Quintus Caecilius Macer, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, lies here.
EDCS-74200164.summary: This is a simple epitaph recording the name, filiation, and tribal affiliation of Quintus Caecilius Macer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200334.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Vibius Acindynus(?), aged 60, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Ulpia, his wife, had it made.
EDCS-74200334.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Divine Manes for Vibius Acindynus(?), aged 60, with his wife Ulpia commissioning the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74200403.translation: Sacred to eternal rest. Tiberius Claudius Eutychus for his most devoted daughter Pubilia Flaviana, aged 26. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-74200403.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Tiberius Claudius Eutychus to his daughter Pubilia Flaviana, who died at age 26, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74400202.translation: Victor, aged 75 years, is laid here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-74400202.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Victor, who was 75 years old, with the standard epitaph 'sit tibi terra levis'.

EDCS-74400207.translation: Calus, son of Tanus, aged 55 years. Pisira and Secunda, his sisters, saw to its construction at their own expense.
EDCS-74400207.summary: A funerary monument erected by sisters Pisira and Secunda for their brother Calus, son of Tanus, who died at age 55.

EDCS-74400599.translation: Titus Julius Silo, aged 35 years, is laid here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-74400599.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Titus Julius Silo, who was 35 years old, with the standard epitaph 'sit tibi terra levis'.

EDCS-75300065.translation: Placidus, tunic of lucnic color. [Fragment with possible numerals].
EDCS-75300065.summary: A fragmented inscription possibly describing the color of a tunic belonging to a person named Placidus, with illegible additions.

EDCS-75300066.summary: A very brief inscription potentially identifying a person named Turai, possibly associated with the place 'Lucnic'.

EDCS-75300067.summary: A short inscription possibly referring to a person named Caesias, with a possible ethnic or place name 'Lucnic'.

EDCS-75300068.summary: A fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of a person, possibly Fabius Niger.

EDCS-75300069.summary: A fragment that appears to record a name, Soricis Ferracinus, followed by illegible text.

EDCS-75300070.summary: An inscription recording the name Cabiton Cirisianus, possibly followed by numerals indicating quantity or a date.

EDCS-75300071.summary: A fragmented inscription that seems to record the name Macri Rhodiacus, possibly referring to someone from Rhodes or of Rhodiac origin.

EDCS-75300072.summary: This inscription likely records a payment or quantity associated with a person named Rufa, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300073.summary: This inscription possibly records a debt or a measurement related to Rusticus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300074.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership marker, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300075.summary: This inscription appears to record quantities of iron or rust, possibly related to trade or inventory, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75600194.translation: Lucius Norbanus Tanginus, son of Aidrenus, of the S[3] tribe, an altar [dedicated to?] BA[3].
EDCS-75600194.summary: This is a dedication of an altar by Lucius Norbanus Tanginus, son of Aidrenus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75600198.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pati[3] Vintius [3], 23.
EDCS-75600198.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pati[3] Vintius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75800003.translation: Arquius, son of Tanginus, lies here.
EDCS-75800003.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording that Arquius, son of Tanginus, is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75900273.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [3]aelio, aged [3] years, [3]vss[1]m, [3]a[3]ensis, saw to its construction. May the earth lie lightly on him.
EDCS-75900273.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an individual named [3]aelio, specifying his age and that a libertus (freedman) saw to his burial, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75900275.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [3]tius, aged 40 years, [3] T. l. Her[3] and Hel[3], freedman, saw to its construction.
EDCS-75900275.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of [3]tius at age 40 and names two freedmen, Her[3] and Hel[3], who arranged for the burial, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75900280.translation: ] VE[1]SI[3] lies here. May the earth lie lightly. Munatia Cha[3] [3]C[3].
EDCS-75900280.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary marker, possibly for an individual named Munatia Cha[3], from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75900282.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aemilia Dynate arranged for the construction of this monument for Titus Aemilius Lupus, aged eighty years; here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-75900282.summary: A tombstone erected by Aemilia Dynate for Titus Aemilius Lupus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-75900435.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Optatinus lived for forty-five years. Cordia, his daughter, placed this monument. [---]
EDCS-75900435.summary: A tombstone erected by Cordia for her father, Optatinus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-75900453.translation: To Jupiter, the Best and Greatest. Festus fulfilled his vow and placed this altar. Nereus.
EDCS-75900453.summary: An altar dedicated to Jupiter by Festus, with Nereus as the dedicator, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-75900454.translation: To Cabura Ladronis, whom he willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-75900454.summary: A dedication to the deity Cabura Ladronis, likely by a freedman or former slave fulfilling a vow, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76100044.translation: Primus, slave of Quintus Cornelius Phoebus.
EDCS-76100044.summary: An epitaph for Primus, a slave of Quintus Cornelius Phoebus, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-76100045.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Fundanus, slave of Lucius Aelius [Censorius?], lived for 11 months and 11 days; here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-76100045.summary: A tombstone for Fundanus, a slave of Lucius Aelius, who died in infancy, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76100046.translation: Proculinus, son of Proculus, from Uxame, aged three years.
EDCS-76100046.summary: An epitaph for Proculinus, son of Proculus, who died at the age of three, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-76100047.summary: An incomplete inscription, possibly an epitaph or dedication, with a partially legible praenomen and name, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-76100048.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Marcius Laetinus, aged fifty years, and for Marcia Eurydice, his wife, aged forty years. Both cremated in the same grave. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Licinius Taurinus and Licinia Didyme arranged for the construction of this monument for their friends.
EDCS-76100048.summary: A tombstone for Marcius Laetinus and his wife Marcia Eurydice, erected by Licinius Taurinus and Licinia Didyme, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76100049.translation: Titus Statulicus Avitus, son of Titus, aged three years; here he lies. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-76100049.summary: A tombstone for Titus Statulicus Avitus, who died at the age of three, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-76100051.translation: To the departed spirits. To Lucius Julius Fortunatus, aged sixty, Claudia Iulia made this for her most devoted husband. Here he lies; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-76100051.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Julius Fortunatus, aged 60, erected by his wife Claudia Iulia, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-76100052.translation: To the departed spirits. Satria Avita, aged fifty, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Her son (?) saw to its construction (?).
EDCS-76100052.summary: A tombstone for Satria Avita, aged 50, with a partial indication that her son arranged the burial, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-76100054.translation: To the departed spirits. Valerius Primus, a [3]tiarius, aged seventy-six, Phrygia dedicated this to her guest. Here he lies; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-76100054.summary: A tombstone for Valerius Primus, aged 76, dedicated by Phrygia, his host/guest-friend, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-76100055.translation: To the departed spirits. [...] aged sixty [...], Pompeia [...] to her husband [...]. Here he lies.
EDCS-76100055.summary: A fragmentary tombstone mentioning a deceased husband and his wife Pompeia, with an age of 60, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-76100056.translation: To the departed spirits. Maria, aged thirteen.
EDCS-76100056.summary: A tombstone for Maria, aged 13, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-76600233.translation: [...] to the Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus, with tribunician power [...].
EDCS-76600233.summary: A fragmentary inscription honoring Emperor Augustus, likely from a public monument, dating to his reign (27 BC - 14 AD).

EDCS-76900066.translation: Alluquius, son of Carnus, fulfilled his vow deservedly.
EDCS-76900066.summary: An inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Alluquius, son of Carnus, likely from a religious or votive context.

EDCS-77100008.summary: A very fragmentary inscription mentioning a woman identified by her father's name (Quintus), origin unknown.

EDCS-77100009.translation: To the departed spirits. [...]stalis [...] lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. Iulia Flaccilla saw to its construction for her most [...]e.
EDCS-77100009.summary: A tombstone for a person named [...]stalis, with arrangements made by Iulia Flaccilla for her relative, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-77100010.summary: A tombstone for Successa Coilia, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-77100014.translation: Here lies, aged 27. May the earth lie lightly on you. Valerius Cosmio, husband, had this made.
EDCS-77100014.summary: A tombstone for a woman aged 27, erected by her husband, dating likely to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100017.translation: Nalla and her son made this. Here he lies. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-77100017.summary: A tombstone for a man, erected by his mother and son, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100018.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [For?] Julia... [age?] 28. Lucius(?) V...
EDCS-77100018.summary: A fragment of a tombstone dedicated to the spirits of the departed, likely for a woman named Julia, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100023.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning a boy, possibly a tombstone, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100024.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly mentioning a veteran, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100026.translation: Tiberius Claudius Columbus made this for himself while alive and for Co...
EDCS-77100026.summary: An inscription recording Tiberius Claudius Columbus making a funerary monument for himself and another person while alive, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100029.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Materna, aged 71, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sinnius Hispanius, heir, had this made.
EDCS-77100029.summary: A tombstone for Materna, aged 71, erected by her heir Sinnius Hispanius, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100033.translation: ... Eutychides, aged 35. Julius Eutyches made this for his most devoted wife. Here she lies. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-77100033.summary: A tombstone for Eutychides, aged 35, erected by her husband Julius Eutyches, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100038.summary: A fragmentary tombstone for Mallia(?) Primitiva, referring to her as a devoted wife, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100039.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning a Marcus Valerius, possibly a tombstone, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100041.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records the name and epithet of a person, possibly a woman, who was pious.

EDCS-77100043.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julia Proba, aged 51. Euticianus, her husband, for his wife...
EDCS-77100043.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Julia Proba, aged 51, and is dedicated by her husband Euticianus.

EDCS-77100046.translation: ...son of Papiria, aged 25. ...son of Papiria, aged 60. Boc...
EDCS-77100046.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to list the names and ages of at least two individuals, identified by their tribe (Papiria) and a gentilicium (Boc).

EDCS-77100058.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vernacula, freedwoman of Plestarchus Dionysius, aged 40 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her husband for his most beloved wife...
EDCS-77100058.summary: This funerary inscription is for Vernacula, a freedwoman, who died at age 40, and includes a wish for the earth to lie lightly upon her.

EDCS-77100065.translation: ...Modestus, freedman, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you...
EDCS-77100065.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription records the burial of Modestus, a freedman, with a common epitaph wishing for light earth.

EDCS-77100066.translation: ...aged 71. Secundilla, her mother, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-77100066.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Secundilla, aged 71, is commemorated by her mother.

EDCS-77100068.translation: ...servant of Felicis, aged 32 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-77100068.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a freedman or slave named Felicis, aged 32, with a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-77100069.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Valerius Vigilius, aged 80, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Aurelius Colonus(?) for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-77100069.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Aurelius Colonus(?) to his brother, Valerius Vigilius, aged 80.

EDCS-77100073.translation: Faustus, slave of Titus Servilius Vettonis, aged 19 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-77100073.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Faustus, a slave of Titus Servilius Vettonis, aged 19.

EDCS-77100079.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Memmius Felix, aged sixty years, for his deserving husband, arranged for this to be made. // He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-77100079.summary: This funerary inscription records that Memmius Felix, aged sixty, was commemorated by his wife, who arranged for the monument.

EDCS-77100084.translation: To the divine shades. Sacred. To Lucius Avercius Celer, veteran of the faithful Seventh Legion Gemina, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe from Italy, Peltonius. Lucius Avercius Maxsimus, his fellow-townsman, to his loving brother. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-77100084.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Lucius Avercius Maxsimus to his brother, Lucius Avercius Celer, a veteran of the Seventh Legion Gemina.

EDCS-77100085.translation: Demetrius of Azzana, here lies. Vicarius, freedman, made [this] for his patron and his own family.
EDCS-77100085.summary: This is a tombstone set up by the freedman Vicarius for his patron and his family, possibly including Demetrius of Azzana.

EDCS-77100088.translation: Chius the house-born slave, aged 23, lies here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-77100088.summary: This is a tombstone for Chius, a slave, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-77100090.translation: ...ionicus of the Aurelii, son of Cari... made [this].
EDCS-77100090.summary: This incomplete inscription records the maker of a monument, possibly related to the Aurelii family.

EDCS-77100092.translation: To the divine shades. Sacred. Lucius Marius Augustinus, aged 75, is buried here. Whose name above the altar, for the state...
EDCS-77100092.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Marius Augustinus, aged 75, with a partial inscription relating to public affairs.

EDCS-77100097.translation: ...icanus. He lies here in the field, 8 feet in the front...
EDCS-77100097.summary: This records the burial place of a person named ...icanus, noting the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-77100105.translation: To the divine shades. Sacred. Severa, daughter of Quintus Ursus Se[verus]...
EDCS-77100105.summary: This is a dedication to the gods of the underworld for Severa, daughter of Quintus Ursus Severus, but the inscription is incomplete.

EDCS-77100112.translation: To the divine shades. Sacred. Meletine, mother, aged 40. Dromas, daughter, aged 20. Gaius Julius Hermes saw to the making of this. They lie here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-77100112.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Gaius Julius Hermes for his mother Meletine (aged 40) and his daughter Dromas (aged 20).

EDCS-77100113.summary: This is a dedication to the gods of the underworld for Secundus, but the inscription is incomplete.

EDCS-77100115.summary: This is a tombstone for Viccia Euphrosyne, who died at the age of 27.

EDCS-77100118.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Pomponia Colona of Emerita, aged 47, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Publius Pomponius Diophanes made this for his dearest wife.
EDCS-77100118.summary: This is a tombstone for Pomponia Colona, erected by her husband Publius Pomponius Diophanes, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100119.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Allia Calliope, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Fabius Maturus made this for his most longed-for wife.
EDCS-77100119.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Allia Calliope, aged 18, set up by her husband Fabius Maturus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100120.translation: Augustinus, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Lasciva placed this from her own funds for her partner.
EDCS-77100120.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Augustinus, aged 25, with the monument erected by his partner Lasciva, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100121.translation: Lucius Baebius Caelianus, freedman of Lucius, left this for his freedmen. Baebia Minerva, freedwoman of Lucius, and Baebia Inventa, freedwoman of Lucius, aged 32, are buried here.
EDCS-77100121.summary: This funerary monument records the burial of Baebia Minerva and Baebia Inventa, freedwomen of Lucius Baebius Caelianus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100122.translation: Marcus Petronius Quadratus, Petronia [---], Marcus Petronius Sa[---], aged 20 [---]
EDCS-77100122.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a tombstone commemorating several individuals, including Marcus Petronius Quadratus and a 20-year-old, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100123.translation: [---] Ursia Vegetta, daughter of Titus, wife, arranged for this to be made from her own funds for her husband and son [---]
EDCS-77100123.summary: This inscription records that Ursia Vegetta funded the construction of a monument for her husband and son, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100125.translation: [---]la Tula, freedwoman, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Iasta placed this from her own funds for her partner.
EDCS-77100125.summary: This tombstone records the burial of a freedwoman named Tula, aged 60, and was erected by her partner Iasta, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100129.summary: This fragmented inscription likely refers to a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning someone as 'most dutiful', dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100130.translation: Cominius [---] from his own funds [---] for his daughter Pompeia [---]
EDCS-77100130.summary: This fragmented inscription indicates that Cominius funded a monument or dedication for his daughter Pompeia, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100132.summary: This is a fragmented inscription beginning with a name and patronymic, likely part of a longer dedication or funerary text, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-77100134.translation: Calpurnia Caesia, daughter of Marcus, thirty-five years of age, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-77100134.summary: A tombstone marking the burial of Calpurnia Caesia, who died at age 35, with a common funerary wish.

EDCS-77100135.translation: Leda Marcia lies here, six years of age.
EDCS-77100135.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Leda Marcia, who died at age 6.

EDCS-77100136.translation: Catellus, slave of Ebrilia Galla, daughter of Caius, fourteen years [old]...
EDCS-77100136.summary: A fragmented tombstone for Catellus, a fourteen-year-old slave belonging to Ebrilia Galla.

EDCS-77100137.translation: To Iunius Veranus, twenty years [of age]...
EDCS-77100137.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication for Iunius Veranus, who died at age 20.

EDCS-77100138.translation: ...Aphrodite had this made for her most dutiful and deserving husband.
EDCS-77100138.summary: A funerary monument where Aphrodite commissioned a tomb for her deserving husband.

EDCS-77100140.summary: A fragmented inscription indicating that someone commissioned a monument.

EDCS-77100142.translation: [...]ntia, freedwoman of Lucius, a pious woman for [her husband/relative] [O]rius, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-77100142.summary: A fragmented tombstone for a freedwoman named [...]ntia, mentioning a pious woman and the common wish for light earth.

EDCS-77100143.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Domitius Pastor, seventy years of age, veteran of the VII Gemina Felix Legion. Titus Munatius(?) Celer(?), soldier of the VII(?) Legion, alive, with his own [means]...
EDCS-77100143.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Domitius Pastor, a veteran of the VII Gemina Felix Legion who died at age 70, with a funerary record by Titus Munatius? Celer?.

EDCS-77100144.translation: Iulia Modesta, daughter of Gaius, seventeen years of age, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-77100144.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Iulia Modesta, who died at age 17, with a common funerary wish.

EDCS-77100145.translation: ... Saturnia the mother, Iunia Silvana the grandmother. May the earth lie lightly upon you all.
EDCS-77100145.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Saturnia the mother and Iunia Silvana the grandmother, with a plural funerary wish.

EDCS-77100146.summary: This is a dedication, likely on a tombstone, by Veniatus Caennonus, with the recipient's name partially lost.

EDCS-77300062.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. [Name lost], son of [Name lost]. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-77300062.summary: This is a funerary inscription with a partially lost epitaph, ending with the common phrase 'sit tibi terra levis'.

EDCS-77300063.translation: Gaius Titius Maurus, aged [lost number] years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Rufus Titius, son of [lost name], took care to have this made at his own expense.
EDCS-77300063.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Titius Maurus, whose age is partially lost, with Rufus Titius arranging its construction.

EDCS-78200065.translation: Paccia Suavis, daughter of Marcus [lost name].
EDCS-78200065.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Paccia Suavis and her father, Marcus.

EDCS-78200066.translation: Rufio Augustinus, son of [lost name], aged 18 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sunnua, his mother, took care to have this made.
EDCS-78200066.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufio Augustinus, who died at 18, arranged by his mother.

EDCS-78200067.translation: [Name lost]estus, freedman of [Name lost]ionis, aged 55 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Terentia Procula took care to have this made for her devoted husband.
EDCS-78200067.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a freedman named [Name lost]estus, arranged by his wife Terentia Procula.

EDCS-78200068.translation: [Name lost]aric, aged 38 years and 7 months, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Aconia Tacita made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-78200068.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a man aged 38 years and 7 months, made by his wife Acconia Tacita.

EDCS-78200073.translation: Publius Licinius Uccio, son of Publius, lies here. Space in front: 20 feet. Space in the field: 15 feet.
EDCS-78200073.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Licinius Uccio, also specifying the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-78200075.translation: Quintus Annnius Silvanus, aged 30 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Titus Annius took care to have this made at his own expense for his brother.
EDCS-78200075.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Annnius Silvanus, arranged by his brother Titus Annius.

EDCS-78200082.translation: Furius Tritanius, aged 85 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Apana Arcai filia, aged 35 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-78200082.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two individuals, Furius Tritanius and Apana, with their ages and parentage.

EDCS-78200083.translation: Atta Materna, of Dingondicus of Uxamis, aged 13, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Valeria Melissa, mother.
EDCS-78200083.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Atta Materna, erected by her mother, dated likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200084.translation: To the departed spirits. [Son's name], son, aged 3, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sunna, mother of Celtus, and Balaesus, father of Tancinus, had this made.
EDCS-78200084.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, erected by his mother and father, dated likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200086.translation: To Dentus, son of Reburrus. Sunna, freedwoman of Primus, had this made for herself and for Dentus.
EDCS-78200086.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription dedicated to Dentus, commissioned by Sunna, his patroness, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-78200087.translation: Gaius Terentius, son of Avitus, of Veleia.
EDCS-78200087.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying a man named Gaius Terentius from Veleia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-79600036.translation: Modestus, son of Paquillus Pyladis, aged 30, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-79600036.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Modestus, erected by his father (implied by the patronymic), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80200125.translation: To the departed spirits. Ianuaria, aged 35, daughter of Euga. Made by her son for his most loving mother, whom he neither remembers nor asked for bread.
EDCS-80200125.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ianuaria, erected by her son who expresses profound grief, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80700059.translation: Aged 40. [Name] B[---], M[---]. Wife [---]. [Husband's name?] had this made.
EDCS-80700059.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the age of a woman and indicating it was commissioned by her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80700158.translation: Boutia, daughter of Ara, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Aged 25. Cadarus had this made for his wife Boutia.
EDCS-80700158.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Boutia, erected by her husband Cadarus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80900080.translation: Paula. L(ocus) P(ublicus)? or L(ocus) P(rivatus)? robber?
EDCS-80900080.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription possibly identifying a person named Paula, with unclear additional text, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80900137.translation: Son of Caenon, who lived 33 years. A[---]lia had this made.
EDCS-80900137.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a man who lived 33 years, erected by a woman named Aelia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81200034.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Claudius Sepi... willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-81200034.summary: An altar dedicated to Jupiter, likely erected by Claudius Sepi in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-81200130.translation: Procilus, aged 51; Boutia, his daughter, aged 3; Venusta, his daughter, aged 2.
EDCS-81200130.summary: A funerary inscription recording the names and ages of deceased individuals, likely family members.

EDCS-81200133.translation: Iulia, daughter of Lubaecus, willingly placed this.
EDCS-81200133.summary: A funerary monument erected by Iulia, daughter of Lubaecus.

EDCS-81200134.translation: Camala Aranti[?], freedwoman, to the Genius... willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-81200134.summary: A votive inscription to a Genius, possibly erected by a freedwoman named Camala.

EDCS-81200136.summary: A very brief inscription likely naming two individuals, possibly related, perhaps marking a grave or property.

EDCS-81200138.translation: Quintus Caecilius Castus, son of Quintus, is buried here. His wife Caecilia Nymphe took care to have this monument made from her own funds for herself, for her husband Quintus Caecilius Castus, son of Quintus, and for Caecilia Gemella, her freedwoman. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-81200138.summary: A detailed funerary inscription for Quintus Caecilius Castus, his wife, daughter, and a freedwoman, prepared by his wife.

EDCS-81200148.summary: A fragmentary inscription that begins to record the name of an individual, likely for a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-81200151.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Flavia... aged 21.
EDCS-81200151.summary: A funerary inscription for a woman named Flavia, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-81200152.translation: Geminus, son of Balaesus, of Tritecum, aged 50. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-81200152.summary: A funerary inscription for Geminus, son of Balaesus, from Tritecum, who died at age 50.

EDCS-81200153.translation: Cloutius Ureni, aged 100. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-81200153.summary: A funerary inscription for Cloutius Ureni, who lived to the remarkable age of 100.

EDCS-81200154.translation: To Flaccus, son of Magilo, 50 years old. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-81200154.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flaccus, son of Magilo, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-81200177.translation: Aper, son of Taganius, 31 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. He made this for his father and mother.
EDCS-81200177.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aper, son of Taganius, who died at 31; it also mentions he made it for his parents.

EDCS-81700056.translation: Gaius Julius Paternus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, lies here.
EDCS-81700056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Paternus.

EDCS-81700058.translation: Fabia, daughter of Lucius. Lucius Fabius Fabianus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, lies here. Father for his daughter.
EDCS-81700058.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia, daughter of Lucius, and Lucius Fabius Fabianus, son of Marcus, likely erected by the latter for his daughter.

EDCS-81900043.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Alluquius, son of [---].

EDCS-82400021.translation: [---], daughter of [---], 45 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Colonus, son of Meduttus, 37 years old. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-82400021.summary: This inscription records two individuals, an unnamed woman of 45 and Colonus, son of Meduttus, aged 37, both buried in the same location.

EDCS-82400022.translation: Tongatius, son of Aconis, 50 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-82400022.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tongatius, son of Aconis, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-82400023.translation: [---] years old. [---] lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sentia Avita (?) placed this for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-82400023.summary: This funerary epitaph records an unnamed individual who died at an unspecified age and was placed by their mother, Sentia Avita (?).

EDCS-82400136.translation: Sacred to the Manes. Vibia Iuliana, 19 years old. Gaius Iuvinius [---] incomparable [---] lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-82400136.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Vibia Iuliana, aged 19, and mentions Gaius Iuvinius, noting her incomparable qualities.

EDCS-82400137.translation: Marcus Cornelius Vegetus, 20 years old, lies here.
EDCS-82400137.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Cornelius Vegetus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-82400138.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Septimia L[...]
EDCS-82400138.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed by Septimia, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82400139.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maria Sicae, aged 40, pious among her own people, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-82400139.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maria Sicae, recording her age and her piety, likely from the Roman Imperial period.
